questions.requirement:
Please describe your loyalty program and its effectiveness with existing customers. Please detail the following in your description: 
 - Operating model (consumer vs. micro business, small/medium business, commercial) 
 - Redemption channels (pay with points, gift cards, cashback, partner transfers, etc.) 
 - Support across multiple channels (phone, online banking, branch, mobile app, email)
 - Communications to customers
questions.bullet:
The Brim platform supports a proprietary, fully integrated Rewards and Loyalty Platform built natively into the credit card platform covering application, underwriting, card issuance, transaction processing, servicing, rewards, and collections. The rewards engine is not a bolt-on or third-party integration. It is embedded directly within the Brim platform, enabling real-time earn and redemption at the transaction level. The platform is live across multiple financial institutions, fintechs, airlines, and merchants in production today.

________________________________________
OPERATING MODEL (Consumer, Micro Business, SMB, Commercial)
________________________________________

Consumer Cards:
- Spend-based rewards with configurable base earn rates
- MCC-category accelerators (e.g., 3X on dining, 2X on groceries)
- Tiered spend thresholds (monthly and annual)
- Milestone bonuses and promotional earn campaigns
- Live with financial institution partners including AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, LAURENTIAN BANK, CWB (CANADIAN WESTERN BANK), and MANULIFE

Micro Business and SMB Cards:
- Rewards structured at the company level with department and employee-level visibility
- Admin controls allow business owners to manage card programs while employees earn rewards on authorized spend
- Accelerated earn on the categories that matter to small businesses: SaaS and software subscriptions, shipping and logistics, office supplies, and advertising spend

Commercial Cards:
- Full commercial card rewards with configurable earn by spend category
- Accelerated rewards on high-frequency business spend: fleet and fuel, car rental, travel and lodging, telecommunications, and SaaS subscriptions
- Spend monitoring, custom reporting, and department-level rewards visibility

Segment-Specific and Partner Programs:
- The Brim platform supports right-sized rewards tailored to specific customer segments, demographics, and partner ecosystems
- ZOLVE: Uses the Brim platform to serve newcomers and new immigrants with rewards tailored to their unique spending patterns and financial needs, structured from basic to premium tiers based on the customer's journey
- MANULIFE: Rewards tied to health and wellness outcomes and insurance product ownership, aligning the card program with Manulife's core demographic and product suite
- AIR FRANCE-KLM FLYING BLUE: Cardholders earn on the Brim platform and burn within the Flying Blue ecosystem for flights, upgrades, and travel experiences, demonstrating how the platform plugs into a third-party airline loyalty portal
- AFFINITY CREDIT UNION: Community-based acceleration where cardholders earn elevated rewards at local merchants and additional bonuses based on cumulative account-level spend
- PAYFACTO: Merchant-specific loyalty program built on the Brim platform, enabling targeted rewards at the point of sale

________________________________________
REDEMPTION CHANNELS
________________________________________

- Pay with Points: Redeem points against any posted transaction directly in the mobile app or online portal (swipe-to-redeem). No POS integration required
- Cashback: Statement credit applied in real-time against the cardholder's balance
- Gift Cards: Virtual and physical gift card fulfillment supported
- Travel Portal: Integrated travel redemption portals. The AIR FRANCE-KLM FLYING BLUE partnership is a live example: cardholders earn on Brim and redeem within Flying Blue for flights, upgrades, and experiences
- Partner Transfers: Earn on Brim, burn on a partner platform (or vice versa). This flexible earn/burn model enables BSB to offer differentiated redemption strategies across agent bank programs
- Charitable Donations: Cardholders can redeem points toward charitable organizations configured by the issuer

________________________________________
MULTI-CHANNEL SUPPORT
________________________________________

- Mobile App (iOS and Android): Full rewards visibility, redemption, offer browsing, and transaction-level detail
- Online Banking Portal: Self-serve rewards dashboard accessible via SSO integration with BSB's existing OLB platform
- Branch Portal: Brim's Enterprise Workflow includes a dedicated Branch Portal enabling BSB staff and agent bank teams to view, manage, and manually adjust cardholder rewards with role-based access controls
- Phone (Call Center): Agent Portal provides customer service representatives with real-time rewards visibility, manual point adjustments, and redemption support
- Email: Automated and configurable rewards communications

________________________________________
COMMUNICATIONS TO CUSTOMERS
________________________________________

- BRIM FINANCIAL supports 150+ distinct notification types delivered to both cardholders and administrators
- Channels: email, SMS, in-app push notifications, and on-statement messaging
- Notifications are triggered in real-time for rewards earn events, redemption confirmations, accelerated earn alerts, milestone achievements, and promotional offers
- All communications are fully white-labeled under BSB's brand (or the agent bank's brand) with configurable messaging, tone, and cadence
- BSB retains full control over which notifications are active, their content, and delivery channel

________________________________________
ONE-TO-MANY AGENT BANK MODEL
________________________________________

- The Brim platform is architected to support BSB's program sponsor model
- Each agent bank partner can be configured with its own unique rewards program with distinct earn structures, merchant partnerships, redemption options, and branded communications
- All managed centrally by BSB through the Brim platform
- This positions BSB competitively as a full-service credit card program sponsor
questions.paragraph:
The Brim platform supports a proprietary, fully integrated Rewards and Loyalty Platform built natively into the credit card platform covering application, underwriting, card issuance, transaction processing, servicing, rewards, and collections. The rewards engine is not a third-party integration. It is embedded directly within the Brim platform, enabling real-time earn and redemption at the transaction level. The platform is live across multiple financial institutions, fintechs, airlines, and merchants in production today.

The platform supports all operating models. Consumer card programs feature spend-based rewards with configurable base earn rates, MCC-category accelerators (for example, 3X on dining or 2X on groceries), tiered spend thresholds at monthly and annual levels, and milestone bonuses. These capabilities are live with financial institution partners including AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, LAURENTIAN BANK, CWB (CANADIAN WESTERN BANK), and MANULIFE. Micro business and SMB card programs structure rewards at the company level with department and employee-level visibility, while admin controls allow business owners to manage programs as employees earn on authorized spend. Accelerated earn is configurable on the categories that matter to small businesses such as SaaS and software subscriptions, shipping and logistics, office supplies, and advertising spend. Commercial card programs deliver rewards on high-frequency business spend including fleet and fuel, car rental, travel and lodging, telecommunications, and SaaS subscriptions, with spend monitoring, custom reporting, and department-level rewards visibility.

The Brim platform supports right-sized rewards tailored to specific customer segments, demographics, and partner ecosystems. ZOLVE uses the Brim platform to serve newcomers and new immigrants with rewards tailored to their unique spending patterns and financial needs, structured from basic to premium tiers based on the customer's journey. MANULIFE ties rewards to health and wellness outcomes and insurance product ownership, aligning the card program with their core demographic and product suite. AIR FRANCE-KLM FLYING BLUE enables cardholders to earn on the Brim platform and burn within the Flying Blue ecosystem for flights, upgrades, and travel experiences, demonstrating how the platform plugs into a third-party airline loyalty portal. AFFINITY CREDIT UNION uses community-based acceleration where cardholders earn elevated rewards at local merchants and additional bonuses based on cumulative account-level spend. PAYFACTO is a live example of merchant-specific loyalty built on the Brim platform, enabling targeted rewards at the point of sale.

Redemption channels include pay-with-points against any posted transaction directly in the mobile app or online portal with no POS integration required, real-time statement credit cashback, virtual and physical gift card fulfillment, travel portal integration (live with AIR FRANCE-KLM FLYING BLUE), partner loyalty transfers supporting earn-on-Brim and burn-elsewhere models, and charitable donations toward issuer-configured organizations.

Multi-channel support spans mobile app on iOS and Android with full rewards visibility and redemption, an online banking portal accessible via SSO integration with BSB's existing OLB platform, a Branch Portal within Brim's Enterprise Workflow enabling BSB staff and agent bank teams to view and manage cardholder rewards with role-based access controls, a Call Center Agent Portal providing real-time rewards visibility and redemption support, and automated email communications.

BRIM FINANCIAL supports over 150 distinct notification types delivered to both cardholders and administrators across email, SMS, in-app push, and on-statement messaging. Notifications are triggered in real-time for earn events, redemption confirmations, accelerated earn alerts, milestone achievements, and promotional offers. All communications are fully white-labeled under BSB's brand or the agent bank's brand, with BSB retaining full control over content, cadence, and delivery channel.

The Brim platform is architected to support BSB's program sponsor model. Each agent bank partner can be configured with its own unique rewards program including distinct earn structures, merchant partnerships, redemption options, and branded communications, all managed centrally by BSB through the Brim platform.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER:
BSB asks for four specific things: operating model across segments, redemption channels, multi-channel support, and customer communications. The response is structured to address each directly using BSB's own terminology. Operating model covers consumer through commercial because BSB's agent bank program will serve all segments. Redemption channels include the earn-on-Brim/burn-elsewhere model (Flying Blue) because this is a key differentiator for agent bank partner programs. Multi-channel maps Brim's portals to BSB's listed channels (phone, OLB, branch, mobile, email).

WHAT IS ASSUMED:
- SSO integration with BSB's OLB and mobile banking will be scoped during implementation
- Agent bank branding and rewards configurations defined during each partner's onboarding
- Rewards communications delivered under BSB or agent bank branding as BSB determines

WHAT IS MISSING: Nothing material. This is well-covered by Brim's existing capabilities and live deployments.

RISK IF WRONG: Low. All capabilities cited are live in production with multiple partners.
questions.notes:
QUESTIONS FOR BRIM:
- Can we share specific engagement metrics (rewards-driven spend lift, activation rates) from AFFINITY CREDIT UNION or Zolve to include as proof points?
- Confirm the 150+ notification type count is accurate for the latest platform release.
- Does Brim have U.S.-specific compliance documentation for rewards programs (state-level escheatment rules for unredeemed points)?
- Is there a maximum number of distinct rewards programs BSB can configure on a single platform instance for the agent bank model?
questions.pricing: Y - Included in base platform pricing.
questions.committee_review:
STRENGTHS: Named client references: Affinity, Affinity Credit Union, Manulife | Uses competitive differentiation language | Response personalised with BSB references
GAPS: No specific numbers or SLA figures — add quantitative proof

questions.requirement: Describe the customization capabilities of the loyalty program. Does your solution support reward scheme definitions and to what levels can reward schemes be defined? How do you differentiate reward types during account setup? Is it possible to apply the scheme across all products, at the product level, for individual cardholders, and at the split household level? Please also define how credit/ debit transactions details are reflected in rewards.
questions.bullet:
A rewards engine with configuration at the program, product, cardholder, and household level that enables BSB to define, customize, and manage reward schemes at every level, from the overall program down to the individual cardholder.

________________________________________
REWARD SCHEME DEFINITIONS
________________________________________

The Brim platform supports reward scheme configuration at the following levels:

- Program Level: Define a master rewards framework that applies across all products and agent bank partners. This establishes the baseline earn/burn structure.
- Product Level: Differentiate reward schemes by card product (e.g., a consumer cashback card earns differently than a business travel card). Each product can have its own base earn rate, accelerator structure, and redemption options.
- Individual Cardholder Level: Override or supplement product-level schemes with cardholder-specific offers such as targeted MCC accelerators, personalized milestone bonuses, or promotional earn rates based on spend behavior.
- Household (Split Household) Level: Rewards can be structured to consolidate under the primary account holder (authorized signer) or split across authorized users. The platform supports household-level earn accumulation where all cards under one account contribute to a shared rewards balance, or alternatively rewards can be allocated proportionally to individual authorized users. This is configurable per program through the admin portal.

________________________________________
DIFFERENTIATING REWARD TYPES DURING ACCOUNT SETUP
________________________________________

- During account setup, BSB (or the agent bank) selects the reward scheme associated with the card product being issued
- The Brim platform allows multiple reward types to coexist: points-based, cashback, hybrid (points + cashback), or partner-currency programs (e.g., airline miles as demonstrated with AIR FRANCE-KLM FLYING BLUE)
- Reward type assignment is driven by the product configuration, ensuring consistency across portfolios while allowing flexibility for promotional or segment-specific variations
- Live examples of differentiated reward types: ZOLVE structures rewards from basic to premium tiers based on the newcomer's journey; MANULIFE ties rewards to health and wellness and insurance product ownership; AFFINITY CREDIT UNION focuses on community-based earn acceleration

________________________________________
HOW CREDIT/DEBIT TRANSACTION DETAILS ARE REFLECTED IN REWARDS
________________________________________

- Rewards are calculated and reflected at the individual transaction level in real-time
- Each posted transaction displays the corresponding points earned (or redeemed) directly in the cardholder's portal and mobile app
- Transaction details include merchant name, MCC code, amount, date, and rewards earned
- For accelerated earn categories (e.g., 3X on dining), the transaction detail clearly indicates the accelerated earn amount
- Debit (return/refund) transactions automatically reverse the corresponding rewards accrual, maintaining accurate rewards balances in real-time

________________________________________
AGENT BANK FLEXIBILITY
________________________________________

- Each agent bank partner under BSB's program can be configured with its own distinct reward scheme definitions
- Example: Agent Bank A may offer a tiered cashback program while Agent Bank B offers a points-based program with local merchant accelerators
- This level of per-partner configurability is live in production today across multiple financial institutions including AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, LAURENTIAN BANK, CWB (CANADIAN WESTERN BANK), and MANULIFE, each with distinct reward structures on the same Brim platform
- All managed centrally by BSB through the Brim platform
questions.paragraph:
A rewards engine with configuration at the program, product, cardholder, and household level that enables BSB to define, customize, and manage reward schemes at every level, from the overall program down to the individual cardholder.

The Brim platform supports reward scheme configuration across four tiers. At the program level, BSB defines a master rewards framework that applies across all products and agent bank partners, establishing the baseline earn and burn structure. At the product level, reward schemes are differentiated by card product so that a consumer cashback card earns differently than a business travel card, with each product having its own base earn rate, accelerator structure, and redemption options. At the individual cardholder level, BSB can override or supplement product-level schemes with cardholder-specific offers such as targeted MCC accelerators, personalized milestone bonuses, or promotional earn rates based on spend behavior. At the household (split household) level, rewards can be structured to consolidate under the primary account holder (authorized signer) or split across authorized users, with the platform supporting household-level earn accumulation where all cards under one account contribute to a shared rewards balance or alternatively rewards allocated proportionally to individual authorized users. This is configurable per program through the admin portal.

During account setup, BSB or the agent bank selects the reward scheme associated with the card product being issued. The Brim platform allows multiple reward types to coexist including points-based, cashback, hybrid (points plus cashback), and partner-currency programs such as airline miles as demonstrated with AIR FRANCE-KLM FLYING BLUE. Reward type assignment is driven by the product configuration, ensuring consistency across portfolios while allowing flexibility for promotional or segment-specific variations. Live examples of differentiated reward types include ZOLVE structuring rewards from basic to premium tiers based on the newcomer's journey, MANULIFE tying rewards to health and wellness and insurance product ownership, and AFFINITY CREDIT UNION focusing on community-based earn acceleration.

Transaction details are reflected in rewards at the individual transaction level in real-time. Each posted transaction displays the corresponding points earned or redeemed directly in the cardholder's portal and mobile app, including merchant name, MCC code, amount, date, and the specific earn calculation applied. For accelerated earn categories the transaction detail clearly indicates the bonus amount. Return and refund transactions automatically reverse the corresponding rewards accrual, maintaining accurate rewards balances in real-time.

Each agent bank partner under BSB's program can be configured with its own distinct reward scheme definitions. This level of per-partner configurability is live in production today across multiple financial institutions including AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, LAURENTIAN BANK, CWB (CANADIAN WESTERN BANK), and MANULIFE, each with distinct reward structures on the same Brim platform. All managed centrally by BSB through the Brim platform.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER:
BSB asks about customization depth: reward scheme definitions and levels, reward type differentiation during setup, applicability across products/individuals/households, and how credit/debit transactions reflect in rewards. The four-tier structure (program, product, cardholder, household) directly mirrors BSB's question about "all products, product level, individual cardholders, and split household level." Marked as "B - Meets with configuration" because capabilities exist but BSB-specific schemes need setup.

WHAT IS ASSUMED:
- Reward scheme definitions per agent bank configured during onboarding
- Split household policies aligned with BSB's program rules during implementation
- No third-party dependencies required

WHAT IS MISSING: Nothing material. Configuration capabilities documented in Manulife RFP.

RISK IF WRONG: Low. Standard configuration exercise.
questions.notes:
QUESTIONS FOR BRIM:
- Confirm that split-household reward allocation (proportional splits to authorized users) is live in production today, or is this a configuration that has been built but not yet deployed with a live partner?
- Can BSB's agent bank administrators self-configure reward scheme changes, or does this require Brim platform team involvement?
- Is there a limit on the number of concurrent reward schemes (product-level configurations) that can be active simultaneously?
questions.pricing: Y - Included in base platform pricing. Reward scheme configuration is a core platform capability.
questions.committee_review:
STRENGTHS: Named client references: Affinity, Affinity Credit Union, Manulife | Uses competitive differentiation language | Response personalised with BSB references
GAPS: No specific numbers or SLA figures — add quantitative proof

questions.requirement: Describe your ability to provide rewards on MCC codes, single merchant specific, single products, as well as your ability to manage relationship with preferred merchant (ex: loyalty).
questions.bullet:
Granular rewards targeting at the MCC code level, individual merchant level, single product level, and preferred merchant relationship level.

________________________________________
MCC CODE-LEVEL REWARDS
________________________________________

- BSB can configure accelerated earn rates on any MCC code or group of MCC codes (e.g., 3X points on all MCC 5812 for Restaurants, or 2X on MCC 5541 for Gas Stations)
- MCC-based accelerators can be set as permanent product features or as time-limited promotional campaigns
- Multiple MCC accelerators can be stacked and combined with other earn rules (e.g., 3X on Dining + 2X on Groceries + 1X on everything else)

________________________________________
SINGLE MERCHANT-SPECIFIC REWARDS
________________________________________

- The Brim platform supports merchant-level targeting down to the individual merchant
- BSB can configure a specific earn rate or bonus for a single named merchant (e.g., "5X points at ABC Family Restaurant" or "Bonus 500 points on first purchase at XYZ Local Hardware")
- Powered by BRIM FINANCIAL's proprietary merchant rewards platform, which enables merchants (local or national) to be onboarded in under 3 minutes with no POS integration required
- Merchant identification is handled through transaction-level data (merchant name, MCC, merchant ID), not through POS hardware
- PAYFACTO is a live example of merchant-specific loyalty built on the Brim platform, enabling targeted rewards tied to individual merchant relationships

________________________________________
SINGLE PRODUCT-LEVEL REWARDS
________________________________________

- Rewards can be configured at the individual card product level
- Example: A BSB "Community Cashback Card" may earn 2% on local spend while a "Travel Rewards Card" earns 3X on travel and dining

________________________________________
PREFERRED MERCHANT RELATIONSHIP MANAGEMENT
________________________________________

- BRIM FINANCIAL operates the Brim Marketplace, a two-sided merchant rewards ecosystem connecting cardholders and merchants, currently supporting 500+ merchant partners
- Merchants can offer issuer-funded or merchant-funded accelerated earn offers, step-up loyalty bonuses (earn more on your 3rd, 5th, 10th visit), and redemption multipliers
- BSB can localize merchant participation by market. For example, onboarding preferred local merchants in Maine communities served by agent bank partners, creating differentiated, community-focused rewards programs
- Merchant onboarding is frictionless: no POS setup, no integration, no upfront fees for the merchant. BRIM FINANCIAL handles all transaction matching and earn calculations on the backend

________________________________________
LIVE EXAMPLES
________________________________________

- AFFINITY CREDIT UNION uses community-based acceleration to reward members for shopping locally. Cardholders earn elevated points at participating local merchants in their communities, and the earn rate increases with cumulative account-level spend, creating a flywheel that drives both card usage and local economic activity
- National brand offers from the Brim Marketplace are available alongside local offers, providing immediate breadth while the institution builds local depth

________________________________________
AGENT BANK ADVANTAGE
________________________________________

- Each agent bank under BSB's program can curate its own preferred merchant network
- This empowers community financial institutions to offer truly local, relationship-driven rewards that deepen customer engagement in their specific markets
questions.paragraph:
Granular rewards targeting at the MCC code level, individual merchant level, single product level, and preferred merchant relationship level. This is a core strength of the Brim platform.

Rewards can also be configured at the individual card product level. For example, a BSB Community Cashback Card may earn 2% on local spend while a Travel Rewards Card earns 3X on travel and dining.

Merchant onboarding is frictionless with no POS setup, no integration, and no upfront fees. BRIM FINANCIAL handles all transaction matching and earn calculations on the backend.

AFFINITY CREDIT UNION uses community-based acceleration to reward members for shopping locally. Cardholders earn elevated points at participating local merchants in their communities, and the earn rate increases with cumulative account-level spend, creating a flywheel that drives both card usage and local economic activity. National brand offers from the Brim Marketplace are available alongside local offers, providing immediate breadth while the institution builds local depth. questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER:
BSB specifically asks about MCC codes, single merchant specific, single products, and ability to manage preferred merchant relationships. The "no POS integration" point is critical because it removes the biggest barrier to local merchant onboarding for BSB's agent bank model. PAYFACTO referenced as proof of merchant-specific loyalty. AFFINITY CREDIT UNION example demonstrates community-based acceleration in production.

WHAT IS ASSUMED:
- Merchant onboarding for BSB-specific local merchants coordinated during program setup
- Merchant-funded offers subject to individual merchant participation agreements
- No POS integration required at any merchant location

WHAT IS MISSING: Confirm whether 500+ merchant count can be disclosed to BSB or should be softened.

RISK IF WRONG: Low for capability. Merchant count disclosure is a marketing decision, not a technical gap.
questions.notes:
QUESTIONS FOR BRIM:
- Can we disclose the 500+ merchant count, or should we soften to "hundreds of merchant partners"?
- Does the merchant onboarding process differ for U.S.-based merchants vs. Canadian merchants? Any regulatory or payment network differences?
- Can agent bank partners onboard merchants independently, or does this require BSB/Brim coordination?
- For the Maine market specifically, has Brim done any preliminary analysis of merchant coverage or would this be entirely new?
questions.pricing: Y - Included in base platform pricing. Merchant onboarding and marketplace capabilities are core features.
questions.committee_review:
STRENGTHS: Named client references: Affinity, Affinity Credit Union, Payfacto | Contains specific numbers or metrics — strong evidence | Uses competitive differentiation language
No significant concerns. Specific, evidenced, and on-target.

questions.requirement: Describe how you manage the merge of accounts and loyalty IDs. Describe how you can share the same Loyalty ID on different accounts. Describe how you use unique loyalty IDs. Can loyalty IDs be created without a customer having a PAN?
questions.bullet:
BRIM FINANCIAL supports loyalty ID management, including account merging, shared loyalty IDs, unique loyalty ID assignment, and loyalty ID creation independent of a PAN.

________________________________________
MERGE OF ACCOUNTS AND LOYALTY IDs
________________________________________

- When accounts are merged (e.g., product upgrade, account consolidation, household changes), the Brim platform consolidates the associated loyalty IDs and rewards balances
- The merged loyalty account retains the full transaction and earn history from both source accounts
- Merge workflows are managed through Brim's Enterprise Workflow portal with role-based access controls and audit trails

________________________________________
SHARED LOYALTY ID ACROSS DIFFERENT ACCOUNTS
________________________________________

- The Brim platform supports linking multiple card accounts to a single shared Loyalty ID
- Use cases include:
 - A customer holding both a personal and business card can accumulate rewards into one shared balance
 - A household with multiple authorized users can pool rewards under a single Loyalty ID tied to the authorized signer
 - An agent bank's business client can link employee cards to a company-level Loyalty ID for consolidated rewards tracking
- BSB defines the rules for which accounts can be linked and how rewards are pooled or split

________________________________________
UNIQUE LOYALTY IDs
________________________________________

- Each cardholder account is assigned a unique, system-generated Loyalty ID upon account creation
- This ID serves as the persistent identifier for all rewards activity (earn, redeem, transfer, reporting) across the lifecycle of the account
- Loyalty IDs are unique across the entire Brim platform, preventing duplication across agent bank programs

________________________________________
LOYALTY ID WITHOUT A PAN
________________________________________

- Yes, the Brim platform supports creating a Loyalty ID without requiring an active PAN
- Use cases:
 - Pre-enrollment: A prospective customer can be issued a Loyalty ID during a marketing campaign or pre-approval process before their card is issued
 - Non-card rewards: Loyalty IDs can be assigned to non-card products (e.g., deposit accounts, loan products) if BSB extends its rewards program beyond credit cards
 - Partner programs: A Loyalty ID can be created for participation in a partner-branded program before a card is activated

________________________________________
AGENT BANK MODEL
________________________________________

- Loyalty IDs are managed within BSB's centralized platform instance, with logical separation by agent bank partner
- Each agent bank's cardholders have unique, trackable loyalty identities while BSB maintains program-level oversight and reporting
questions.paragraph:
BRIM FINANCIAL supports loyalty ID management, including account merging, shared loyalty IDs, unique loyalty ID assignment, and loyalty ID creation independent of a PAN.

When accounts are merged due to product upgrades, consolidation, or household changes, the Brim platform consolidates the associated loyalty IDs and rewards balances while retaining the full transaction and earn history from both source accounts. Merge workflows are managed through Brim's Enterprise Workflow portal with role-based access controls and full audit trails.

The platform supports linking multiple card accounts to a single shared Loyalty ID. This enables scenarios where a customer holding both a personal and business card accumulates rewards into one shared balance, where a household with multiple authorized users pools rewards under a single Loyalty ID tied to the authorized signer, or where an agent bank's business client links employee cards to a company-level Loyalty ID for consolidated rewards tracking. BSB defines the rules for which accounts can be linked and how rewards are pooled or split.

Each cardholder account is assigned a unique, system-generated Loyalty ID upon account creation. This ID serves as the persistent identifier for all rewards activity including earn, redeem, transfer, and reporting across the lifecycle of the account. Loyalty IDs are unique across the entire Brim platform, preventing duplication across agent bank programs.

The Brim platform supports creating a Loyalty ID without requiring an active PAN. This enables pre-enrollment during marketing campaigns before a card is issued, assignment to non-card products such as deposit accounts or loan products if BSB extends rewards beyond credit cards, and participation in partner-branded programs before card activation.

Loyalty IDs are managed within BSB's centralized platform instance with logical separation by agent bank partner. Each agent bank's cardholders have unique, trackable loyalty identities while BSB maintains program-level oversight and reporting.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER:
BSB asks four distinct sub-questions: merging accounts/loyalty IDs, sharing same loyalty ID across accounts, unique loyalty IDs, and creating IDs without PAN. Each addressed with its own section. Non-PAN loyalty ID highlighted because it opens the door for BSB to extend rewards beyond credit cards.

WHAT IS ASSUMED:
- Account merge rules and linking policies defined by BSB during configuration
- Non-PAN Loyalty ID use cases (deposit account rewards) require scoping

WHAT IS MISSING:
- Confirm non-PAN Loyalty ID has been deployed with a live partner or is built but not yet activated
- Confirm merge workflow preserves full earn/redemption history with no data loss
- Clarify if merge is self-serve through Enterprise Workflow or requires Brim support ticket

ACTION BEFORE APRIL 10: Get Brim product team to confirm non-PAN Loyalty ID deployment status.
questions.notes:
QUESTIONS FOR BRIM:
- Has non-PAN Loyalty ID creation been deployed with a live partner, or is this a supported capability not yet activated in production?
- Confirm the Loyalty ID merge workflow preserves full earn/redemption history from both source accounts with no data loss.
- What is the technical process for executing an account merge? Self-serve through Enterprise Workflow or support ticket to Brim?
questions.pricing: Y - Included in base platform pricing.
questions.committee_review:
STRENGTHS: Uses competitive differentiation language | Response personalised with BSB references
GAPS: No named client references — add Affinity CU, Zolve, or Continental as proof points | No specific numbers or SLA figures — add quantitative proof | WEAK EVIDENCE: Response could be stronger with client references or metrics

questions.requirement: Describe how your solution manages householding of rewards. Can rewards be split out to authorized users as well as the authorized signer? Describe how you support reward schemes with co-branded partner participation (extra information provided to partners, specific fee structure, etc.).  How would you provide this support through another program like Mastercard Rewards Solutions (MRS)? Please describe the features, if applicable.
questions.bullet:
BRIM FINANCIAL runs householding of rewards, authorized user reward allocation, partner-branded programs, and third-party rewards integration in production today across multiple financial institutions.

________________________________________
HOUSEHOLDING OF REWARDS
________________________________________

- All cards under a single account (primary cardholder and authorized users) contribute to one shared rewards balance — this is the default behavior, live across every Brim-powered program today
- Rewards accumulate in real-time across all household cards
- Step-up loyalty tiers are calculated on cumulative household spend (e.g., when total monthly household spend crosses $2,000, the earn rate steps up from 1X to 1.5X)

________________________________________
SPLITTING REWARDS TO AUTHORIZED USERS
________________________________________

Three models are live and selectable per product:
- Consolidated Model: All rewards pool under the primary cardholder's account. Authorized users see their individual earn contributions; redemption is controlled by the authorized signer. This is how AFFINITY CREDIT UNION runs today.
- Split Model: Each authorized user earns and accumulates their own balance. The authorized signer retains visibility and override control.
- Hybrid Model: Base earn consolidates under the household; bonus or promotional earn accrues to the individual card that triggered the transaction.

________________________________________
PARTNER-BRANDED PROGRAM SUPPORT
________________________________________

- Live with AIR FRANCE-KLM FLYING BLUE: Cardholders earn Flying Blue Miles on everyday purchases through the Brim platform and redeem within the Flying Blue ecosystem for flights, upgrades, and experiences. This is a production deployment, not a concept.
- Live with MANULIFE: Rewards tied to health and wellness outcomes and insurance product ownership — cross-product reward structures operating at scale with 55K+ financial advisors on the platform.
- Live with LAURENTIAN BANK and CWB: Enterprise-wide rewards extending beyond the credit card product, linking rewards to the broader banking relationship.
- Data feeds to brand partners include transaction volumes, earn/burn activity, cardholder engagement metrics, and promotional performance data (subject to BSB's data governance policies)
- Fee structures support partner-specific interchange sharing, merchant-funded earn contributions, and hybrid models

________________________________________
MASTERCARD REWARDS SOLUTIONS (MRS) AND THIRD-PARTY INTEGRATION
________________________________________

- Brim's proprietary rewards engine is the primary platform — 300+ RESTful APIs make third-party integration straightforward
- The API layer supports MRS and other external earn/burn platforms
- Integration works both directions: earn on Brim / burn on a third-party platform, or earn externally / burn on Brim

________________________________________
AGENT BANK FLEXIBILITY
________________________________________

- Each agent bank runs its own householding rules, authorized user reward policies, and partner-branded integrations — live today with AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, LAURENTIAN BANK, CWB, and MANULIFE operating different configurations on the same platform
- All managed centrally by BSB through the Brim platform
questions.paragraph:
BRIM FINANCIAL runs householding of rewards, authorized user allocation, partner-branded programs, and third-party rewards integration in production today.

All cards under a single account — primary cardholder and authorized users — contribute to one shared household rewards balance. This is the default behavior, live across every Brim-powered program. Rewards accumulate in real-time, and step-up loyalty tiers are calculated on cumulative household spend (e.g., when total monthly household spend crosses $2,000, the earn rate steps from 1X to 1.5X).

BSB selects from three live models for authorized user rewards. The consolidated model pools all rewards under the primary cardholder, with authorized users seeing their contributions but redemption controlled by the authorized signer — this is how AFFINITY CREDIT UNION runs today. The split model gives each authorized user their own balance, with the authorized signer retaining visibility and override control. The hybrid model consolidates base earn under the household while bonus earn accrues to the individual card that triggered the transaction. Each model is selectable per product or per agent bank program.

Partner-branded programs are a proven, live capability. AIR FRANCE-KLM FLYING BLUE cardholders earn on the Brim platform and redeem within the Flying Blue ecosystem — a production deployment running today. MANULIFE ties rewards to health and wellness outcomes and insurance product ownership, operating at scale with 55K+ financial advisors on the platform. LAURENTIAN BANK and CWB run enterprise-wide rewards linking card programs to the broader banking relationship. Brim operates its own proprietary rewards engine and supports integration with Mastercard Rewards Solutions (MRS) and other external platforms through its 300+ RESTful APIs. Each agent bank runs its own configuration — AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, LAURENTIAN BANK, CWB, and MANULIFE each operate different reward structures on the same platform, all managed centrally by BSB.

questions.requirement: How are rewards earned, distributed, and reflected? Are earned rewards distributed daily or at statement cycle (vs. at end of month)? How do you support confirmation that customers received their rewards? Does your system contain cut-off dates that would delay a system or statement from reflecting points earned to a later statement date (e.g., transactions that occur 3 days prior to end of statement cycle)?
questions.bullet:
BRIM FINANCIAL processes rewards in real-time at the transaction level, with no batch delays, no end-of-cycle processing, and no cut-off date issues.

________________________________________
HOW REWARDS ARE EARNED
________________________________________

- Rewards are earned on a per-transaction basis
- When a purchase transaction is authorized and posted (cleared), the Brim platform calculates the applicable earn including base earn, MCC accelerators, merchant-specific bonuses, and tiered spend incentives, then credits the points to the cardholder's rewards balance in real-time
- Earn calculations account for all active rules simultaneously: base rate + category accelerators + merchant offers + promotional campaigns + tier bonuses

________________________________________
HOW REWARDS ARE DISTRIBUTED
________________________________________

- Earned rewards are distributed in real-time upon transaction posting, not at statement cycle or end of month
- The cardholder's rewards balance updates immediately as each transaction posts
- This reinforces engagement and card usage as cardholders see rewards grow throughout the billing cycle

________________________________________
HOW REWARDS ARE REFLECTED
________________________________________

- Mobile App and Online Portal: Real-time rewards balance and per-transaction earn detail visible immediately
- Statements: Monthly statements include a Rewards Summary section showing opening balance, points earned, points redeemed, and closing balance for the period
- Notifications: Configurable email, SMS, and in-app push notifications confirm earn events. Accelerated earn events trigger specific notifications alerting the cardholder to the additional value earned

________________________________________
CONFIRMATION THAT CUSTOMERS RECEIVED REWARDS
________________________________________

- In-app transaction detail showing points earned per transaction
- Push notification or email notification for accelerated or bonus earn events
- Monthly statement rewards summary with full earn/redeem detail
- BSB and agent bank administrators can view cardholder rewards activity through Brim's Enterprise Workflow portal

________________________________________
CUT-OFF DATES
________________________________________

- The Brim platform does NOT impose cut-off dates that would delay rewards from appearing on a statement
- Rewards are calculated and credited in real-time upon transaction posting
- All transactions that post within a statement cycle have their corresponding rewards reflected on that same statement, regardless of when in the cycle the transaction occurred
- This eliminates the common pain point with legacy rewards processors where transactions 3-5 days before cycle close may have rewards deferred to the following period
questions.paragraph:
BRIM FINANCIAL processes rewards in real-time at the transaction level, with no batch delays, no end-of-cycle processing, and no cut-off date issues.

Rewards are earned on a per-transaction basis. When a purchase transaction is authorized and subsequently posted, the Brim platform calculates all applicable earn rules simultaneously including base earn, MCC accelerators, merchant-specific bonuses, and tiered spend incentives, then credits points to the cardholder's balance in real-time. Earned rewards are distributed upon transaction posting, not at statement cycle or end of month. The cardholder's rewards balance updates immediately as each transaction posts, reinforcing engagement as they see rewards grow throughout the billing cycle.

Earned rewards are reflected in three places simultaneously. In the mobile app and online portal, cardholders see real-time rewards balances and per-transaction earn detail immediately. On monthly statements, a Rewards Summary section shows opening balance, points earned, points redeemed, and closing balance for the period. Through configurable notifications via email, SMS, and in-app push, earn events are confirmed in real-time with specific alerts for accelerated earn (for example, when a merchant bonus is triggered).

Cardholders receive confirmation of rewards through in-app transaction detail, push or email notifications for bonus earn events, and the monthly statement rewards summary. BSB and agent bank administrators can view cardholder rewards activity through Brim's Enterprise Workflow portal for servicing and verification.

The Brim platform does not impose cut-off dates that would delay rewards from appearing on a statement. Because rewards are calculated and credited in real-time upon transaction posting, all transactions within a statement cycle have their corresponding rewards reflected on that same statement regardless of when in the cycle the transaction occurred. This eliminates the common legacy-system issue where transactions occurring 3 to 5 days before cycle close have rewards deferred to the following period.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER:
BSB asks how rewards are earned/distributed/reflected, whether distribution is daily or at statement cycle, customer confirmation, and cut-off dates. "Real-time at the transaction level" is the core differentiator. BSB references "transactions that occur 3 days prior to end of statement cycle" which indicates concern about legacy lag. The no-cut-off-date point is given extra emphasis because it directly addresses this pain point.

WHAT IS ASSUMED: None. Real-time processing is inherent to the platform architecture.

WHAT IS MISSING: Nothing material.

RISK IF WRONG: Low. This is core platform architecture, not a configuration.
questions.notes:
QUESTIONS FOR BRIM:
- The "no cut-off date" claim is a strong differentiator. Can Brim provide a technical brief or architecture diagram for the on-site demo?
- Confirm there are zero edge cases where rewards might not appear on the same statement as the triggering transaction (e.g., late-posting international transactions, disputed transactions pending resolution).
- What is the actual latency between transaction posting and rewards balance update? Sub-second? Under 5 seconds?
questions.pricing: Y - Included in base platform pricing. Real-time rewards processing is a core platform capability.

GAPS: No named client references — add Affinity CU, Zolve, or Continental as proof points

questions.requirement: If you provide a rewards platform, how do you ensure rewards accrual/redemption is in real-time sync with purchasing/clearing events? How do you support a variety of redemption items and quick delivery of rewards (e.g., physical gift card delivery instant or ≤ 5 minutes worst case) and near-real-time points balance updates (e.g., rewards balance updates ≤ 5 minutes from posting). Describe how you can integrate with the 3rd party for rewards fulfillment. Can this data be shared in real-time?
questions.bullet:
BRIM FINANCIAL ensures real-time synchronization between rewards accrual/redemption and purchasing/clearing events, with near-instant balance updates and flexible fulfillment.

________________________________________
REAL-TIME SYNC (ACCRUAL AND REDEMPTION)
________________________________________

- The rewards engine is natively integrated into the transaction processing pipeline
- No batch process, no overnight sync, and no separate rewards processor to reconcile against
- Refund/return transactions automatically reverse the corresponding rewards accrual in real-time

________________________________________
VARIETY OF REDEMPTION ITEMS
________________________________________

- Pay with Points on any transaction: Redeem against any posted purchase in the mobile app or portal
- Statement Credit / Cashback: Redeem as a credit against the statement balance at any time
- Gift Cards: Virtual gift card delivery supported with near-instant fulfillment. Physical gift card fulfillment supported through configurable workflows
- Travel: Integrated travel redemption portals (proven with Air France-KLM Flying Blue). BSB can offer travel-based redemption through a partner portal or Brim's configurable framework
- Merchandise: Redemption against any transaction effectively enables merchandise redemption. Cardholders purchase what they want and redeem points against that transaction
- Charitable Donations: Points redeemed toward configured charitable organizations
- Partner Transfer: Points transferred to partner loyalty programs (earn on Brim, burn elsewhere)

________________________________________
QUICK DELIVERY OF REWARDS
________________________________________

- Virtual gift cards: Delivered instantly (digital fulfillment)
- Physical gift cards: Configurable based on BSB's fulfillment partner; standard 5-7 business days, expedited options available
- Statement credit / cashback: Applied in real-time
- Transaction-level redemption: Processed in real-time

________________________________________
NEAR-REAL-TIME POINTS BALANCE UPDATES
________________________________________

- Rewards balance updates occur within seconds of a transaction posting or redemption event
- This exceeds the 5-minute threshold referenced in BSB's requirement
- Cardholders see their updated balance immediately in the mobile app and online portal

________________________________________
THIRD-PARTY REWARDS FULFILLMENT INTEGRATION
________________________________________

- API-first architecture supports integration with third-party fulfillment providers for gift card networks, travel booking engines, and merchandise catalogs
- Data sharing with fulfillment partners configurable in real-time via API or scheduled batch
- Proven with integrations such as Flying Blue for Air France-KLM

________________________________________
AGENT BANK FLEXIBILITY
________________________________________

- Redemption options and fulfillment configurations can be customized per agent bank
questions.paragraph:
BRIM FINANCIAL ensures real-time synchronization between rewards accrual, redemption, and purchasing/clearing events with near-instant balance updates and flexible fulfillment options.

The rewards engine is natively integrated into the transaction processing pipeline with no batch process, no overnight sync, and no separate rewards processor to reconcile against. Refund and return transactions automatically reverse the corresponding rewards accrual in real-time, maintaining a synchronized and accurate rewards ledger at all times.

Redemption options include pay-with-points against any posted transaction in the mobile app or portal, statement credit cashback applied in real-time, virtual gift cards delivered instantly, physical gift cards fulfilled through configurable workflows with standard 5 to 7 business day delivery and expedited options, travel redemption through integrated partner portals (proven with Air France-KLM Flying Blue), merchandise redemption against any transaction (cardholders purchase what they want and redeem points against that transaction), charitable donations toward configured organizations, and partner loyalty transfers supporting the earn-on-Brim, burn-elsewhere model.

Rewards balance updates occur within seconds of a transaction posting or redemption event, exceeding the 5-minute threshold referenced in BSB's requirement. Cardholders see their updated balance immediately in the mobile app and online portal.

The Brim platform's API-first architecture supports integration with third-party fulfillment providers for gift card networks, travel booking engines, and merchandise catalogs. Data sharing with fulfillment partners is configurable in real-time via API or scheduled batch depending on partner capabilities. Redemption options and fulfillment configurations can be customized per agent bank, enabling BSB to tailor the experience for each partner's market.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER:
BSB asks about real-time sync, variety of redemption items, quick delivery (gift card in 5 minutes), near-real-time balance updates (under 5 minutes), and third-party fulfillment. Virtual gift cards meet the 5-minute target. Physical gift cards cannot meet a 5-minute delivery for obvious logistics reasons. Transparent about this.

WHAT IS ASSUMED:
- Physical gift card fulfillment timelines depend on BSB's fulfillment partner
- Travel portal or catalog integration scoped during implementation if needed
- Virtual gift card and statement credit available out of the box

WHAT IS MISSING:
- Does Brim have a US fulfillment logistics partner for physical gift cards?
- BSB says "physical gift card delivery instant or 5 minutes." Could this mean in-branch instant issuance at a kiosk? Need clarification.
- Beyond Flying Blue, any other travel booking engine integrations?

ACTION BEFORE APRIL 10: Clarify BSB's intent on "instant" physical gift card delivery. Confirm US fulfillment partner.
questions.notes:
QUESTIONS FOR BRIM:
- Physical gift card fulfillment: does Brim have an existing fulfillment logistics partner for the U.S., or would BSB need to procure their own? Standard SLA?
- For the "within seconds" balance update claim, is there monitoring or SLA data we can share to substantiate?
- Beyond Flying Blue, has Brim integrated with any other travel booking engines or would this be purpose-built for BSB?
- BSB says "physical gift card delivery instant or 5 minutes worst case." This seems to reference in-branch instant issuance. Does Brim support printing gift cards at a branch or kiosk?

questions.requirement: Describe how different reward conversion rates can be applied to different product types. Describe how your system supports "cashback" (redeeming cashback for travel, gift cards, statement credit, etc.). What parameters can the bank set around cashback redemption?
questions.bullet:
BRIM FINANCIAL supports configurable through the Brim admin portal reward conversion rates by product type and cashback functionality with bank-defined parameters.

________________________________________
DIFFERENT REWARD CONVERSION RATES BY PRODUCT TYPE
________________________________________

- BSB can define distinct reward conversion (earn) rates for each card product:
 - Consumer Cashback Card: 1.5% cashback on all purchases, 3% on groceries
 - Consumer Rewards Card: 1 point per $1 on all purchases, 3X points on dining, 2X on travel
 - Business Card: 2 points per $1 on SaaS subscriptions, shipping and logistics, and advertising spend; 1 point per $1 on everything else
 - Commercial Card: Accelerated earn on fleet and fuel, car rental, travel and lodging, and telecommunications; flat 1 point per $1 on remaining spend
- Conversion rates are configurable at the product level, the cardholder segment level, and can include time-limited promotional rates
- Each agent bank can define its own conversion rate structure per product

________________________________________
CASHBACK SUPPORT
________________________________________

- Statement Credit: Cardholders redeem points as a credit applied directly to their statement balance, initiated at any time (not restricted to end-of-month or year-end)
- Transaction-Level Redemption: Cardholders select a specific posted transaction and redeem points against it, functioning as cashback on a specific purchase
- Travel Redemption: Points redeemed against travel-related transactions or through an integrated travel portal
- Gift Card Redemption: Points redeemed for virtual or physical gift cards from configured merchants or networks
- Partner Transfer: Points transferred to a partner loyalty program at a configured conversion rate
- Charitable Donations: Points redeemed toward configured charitable organizations

________________________________________
BANK-CONFIGURABLE PARAMETERS FOR CASHBACK
________________________________________

- Minimum Redemption Threshold: Minimum points required before redemption is allowed
- Redemption Rate (Burn Rate): Dollar value per point for cashback redemptions. Different burn rates can be set per redemption channel.
- Redemption Frequency Limits: How often a cardholder can redeem (e.g., once per day, per cycle, or unlimited)
- Maximum Redemption per Period: Cap on total points or dollar value redeemable per billing cycle or year
- Eligible Transaction Types: Define which transactions are eligible for points-against-transaction redemption (e.g., exclude cash advances, balance transfers, fees)
- Amplified Redemption: Higher-value redemption rates at select merchants (e.g., "Your points are worth 2X when redeemed at a participating local merchant")
- Delinquent Account Controls: Automatic suspension of earn and redemption for delinquent accounts with configurable reinstatement rules
questions.paragraph:
BRIM FINANCIAL supports configurable through the Brim admin portal reward conversion rates by product type and cashback functionality with bank-defined parameters.

BSB can define distinct reward conversion rates for each card product in the portfolio. For example, a consumer cashback card could earn 1.5% on all purchases with 3% on groceries, a consumer rewards card could earn 1 point per dollar on all purchases with 3X on dining and 2X on travel, a business card could earn 2 points per dollar on SaaS subscriptions, shipping and logistics, and advertising spend with 1 point on everything else, and a commercial card could offer accelerated earn on fleet and fuel, car rental, travel and lodging, and telecommunications with a flat 1 point per dollar on remaining spend. Conversion rates are configurable at the product level and the cardholder segment level and can include time-limited promotional rates. Each agent bank can define its own conversion rate structure per product.

The Brim platform supports cashback as a core redemption option through several mechanics. Statement credit allows cardholders to redeem points as a credit applied directly to their balance at any time, not restricted to end-of-month or year-end. Transaction-level redemption lets cardholders select a specific posted transaction and redeem against it, functioning as cashback on a specific purchase. Additional channels include travel redemption against travel transactions or through an integrated portal, gift card redemption for virtual or physical cards, partner transfer at configured conversion rates, and charitable donations toward configured organizations.

BSB retains full control over cashback parameters including minimum redemption thresholds, redemption rates (burn rates) that can differ by channel, frequency limits, maximum redemption per period, eligible transaction types (with the ability to exclude cash advances, balance transfers, or fees), amplified redemption at select merchants where points are worth more, and automatic suspension of earn and redemption for delinquent accounts with configurable reinstatement rules.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER:
BSB asks about different conversion rates by product and cashback parameters. Product examples map to card types BSB would offer. Seven specific cashback parameters listed because BSB asked "What parameters can the bank set?" This detail signals BSB would have real control over economics.

WHAT IS ASSUMED:
- Cashback parameters defined by BSB during program setup
- Amplified redemption merchant participation subject to merchant agreements

WHAT IS MISSING: Confirm amplified redemption at select merchants is live, and confirm different burn rates can be set per redemption channel.

RISK IF WRONG: Low. Standard configuration.
questions.notes:
QUESTIONS FOR BRIM:
- Amplified redemption at select merchants: is this currently live with any partner, or configurable but not yet activated?
- Confirm BSB can set different burn rates for different redemption channels (e.g., 1 point = $0.01 for statement credit, but 1 point = $0.015 for merchant-specific redemption).
- Automatic delinquent account suspension: what is the configurable trigger (days past due, status code, manual flag)?

GAPS: No named client references — add Affinity CU, Zolve, or Continental as proof points

questions.requirement: Do you provide a personalized rewards tracker. How do you enable the rewards program to show the dollar amount to be calculated by the financial institution? Describe how your points portal is dynamic and modern, displaying valid information to customers in the moment (e.g., points should accumulate as the customer spends).
questions.bullet:
The Brim platform delivers a personalized, dynamic, and modern rewards tracker that gives cardholders real-time visibility into their rewards activity and balance.

________________________________________
PERSONALIZED REWARDS TRACKER
________________________________________

Every cardholder has a dedicated Rewards Dashboard in the mobile app and online portal displaying:
- Current rewards balance (points and equivalent dollar value)
- Points earned this statement period vs. prior periods
- Per-transaction earn detail (base earn + any accelerators/bonuses applied)
- Full earn and redemption history with date, amount, merchant, and earn type
- Active offers and accelerated earn opportunities from the Brim Marketplace
- Progress toward tiered spend thresholds (e.g., "Spend $200 more this month to unlock Level 3 earn rates")

________________________________________
DOLLAR AMOUNT CALCULATION BY THE FINANCIAL INSTITUTION
________________________________________

- BSB defines the points-to-dollar conversion rate (burn rate) within the Brim platform
- The dollar value is displayed alongside the points balance so customers always see both their points and the equivalent dollar value
- Example: If BSB sets 1 point = $0.01, a cardholder with 10,000 points sees "10,000 points ($100.00 value)"
- The dollar value updates dynamically as points are earned and redeemed
- If BSB configures amplified redemption at select merchants, the portal displays both standard and amplified values

________________________________________
DYNAMIC AND MODERN POINTS PORTAL
________________________________________

- Points accumulate as the customer spends, in real-time. No delay between transaction posting and rewards balance updating
- Real-time balance updates visible immediately upon transaction posting
- Interactive transaction feed with swipe-to-redeem functionality in the mobile app
- Visual spend breakdowns by category, merchant, and time period
- Gamified elements including monthly rewards summaries, current tier level, and progress toward the next reward level
- Offer tiles from the Brim Marketplace showing available accelerated earn and redemption opportunities
- Fully white-labeled under BSB's brand (or the agent bank's brand) and accessible via SSO

________________________________________
LIVE EXAMPLE
________________________________________

- AFFINITY CREDIT UNION members access a branded rewards portal powered by BRIM FINANCIAL where they view real-time points balances, browse local and national merchant offers, and redeem directly from their transaction history
- The same experience is configurable for BSB and each of its agent bank partners
questions.paragraph:
The Brim platform delivers a personalized, dynamic, and modern rewards tracker that gives cardholders real-time visibility into their rewards activity and balance, designed to drive engagement and card utilization.

Every cardholder has a dedicated Rewards Dashboard within the mobile app and online portal. This dashboard displays the current rewards balance in both points and equivalent dollar value, points earned in the current statement period compared to prior periods, per-transaction earn detail showing base earn and any accelerators or bonuses applied, a full earn and redemption history with date, amount, merchant, and earn type, active offers and accelerated earn opportunities from the Brim Marketplace, and progress toward tiered spend thresholds such as how much more the cardholder needs to spend to unlock the next earn level.

BSB defines the points-to-dollar conversion rate within the Brim platform, and this value is displayed dynamically alongside the points balance so customers always see both metrics. For example, if BSB sets the burn rate at 1 point equals $0.01, a cardholder with 10,000 points sees "10,000 points ($100.00 value)" in their dashboard. The dollar value calculation updates in real-time as points are earned and redeemed. Where amplified redemption is configured at select merchants, the portal displays both the standard value and the amplified value.

Points accumulate as the customer spends in real-time with no delay between transaction posting and balance update. The portal features an interactive transaction feed with swipe-to-redeem functionality in the mobile app, visual spend breakdowns by category and merchant, gamified elements including monthly rewards summaries and tier progress indicators, and offer tiles from the Brim Marketplace. The entire experience is fully white-labeled under BSB's brand or the agent bank's brand and accessible via SSO from BSB's online banking environment.

AFFINITY CREDIT UNION members access a branded rewards portal powered by BRIM FINANCIAL where they view real-time points balances, browse local and national merchant offers, and redeem directly from their transaction history. The same experience is configurable for BSB and each of its agent bank partners.

AFFINITY CREDIT UNION grounds it in a live deployment.

questions.requirement: Describe your rewards reconciliation process and mechanics (e.g., DDA deposits vs cash back on statement) and how accounting can reconcile these items from a tracking perspective. How does the platform handle end-to-end rewards reconciliation or provide integration to a rewards engine?
questions.bullet:
Full-lifecycle rewards reconciliation with full transparency into earn, redemption, and settlement mechanics.

________________________________________
REWARDS RECONCILIATION PROCESS AND MECHANICS
________________________________________

- The Brim platform maintains a real-time rewards ledger for every cardholder account tracking all earn events (transaction-level), redemption events, adjustments, expirations (if configured), and transfers
- Available through Brim's reporting suite (100+ standard reports covering operations, risk, credit, rewards, customer engagement, and financial performance)
- Reconciliation data available in daily, weekly, monthly, and on-demand reporting cycles

________________________________________
DDA DEPOSITS VS. CASHBACK ON STATEMENT
________________________________________

- Statement Credit: Credit applied directly to the card balance. Appears as a line item ("Rewards Redemption - Statement Credit") and reduces the amount due. From an accounting perspective, this is a contra-revenue entry against the rewards liability.
- DDA Deposit: If BSB supports direct deposit of cashback into a cardholder's bank account, the Brim platform facilitates this through configurable settlement workflows. The redemption is recorded in the rewards ledger and the corresponding deposit instruction is generated for BSB's core banking system.
- Both types tracked separately, enabling independent reconciliation

________________________________________
ACCOUNTING RECONCILIATION AND TRACKING
________________________________________

- Daily rewards accrual reports: Total points earned across the portfolio, by product, by agent bank, and by card
- Daily redemption reports: Total points redeemed, broken down by channel (statement credit, DDA deposit, gift card, transaction-level, partner transfer, charitable donation)
- Rewards liability reports: Outstanding unredeemed points balance for balance sheet management
- Settlement reconciliation files: Machine-readable files mapping rewards activity to accounting entries for GL import
- Agent bank-level reporting: Rewards activity segmented by agent bank partner for per-partner P&L tracking
- All reports accessible through the Enterprise Workflow portal and exportable in standard formats (CSV, Excel) or delivered via automated SFTP feeds

________________________________________
PLATFORM-NATIVE INTEGRATION
________________________________________

- Because the rewards engine is natively built into the Brim platform (not a third-party bolt-on), there is no reconciliation gap between the transaction processor and the rewards engine
- Earn, redemption, and settlement are managed within one integrated system
- If BSB requires integration with an external rewards engine for specific programs, the API-first architecture supports real-time data exchange
questions.paragraph:
Full-lifecycle rewards reconciliation with full transparency into earn, redemption, and settlement mechanics, enabling BSB's accounting teams to reconcile rewards activity accurately.

The Brim platform maintains a real-time rewards ledger for every cardholder account tracking all earn events at the transaction level, redemption events, adjustments, expirations if configured, and transfers. This data is available through Brim's reporting suite which includes over 100 standard reports covering operations, risk, credit, rewards, customer engagement, and financial performance. Reconciliation data is available in daily, weekly, monthly, and on-demand reporting cycles.

For statement credit cashback, the credit is applied directly to the card balance and appears as a line item on the statement. From an accounting perspective this is a contra-revenue entry against the rewards liability. For DDA deposits, the Brim platform facilitates direct deposit of cashback into a cardholder's bank account through configurable settlement workflows. The redemption is recorded in the rewards ledger and the corresponding deposit instruction is generated for BSB's core banking system. Both redemption types are tracked separately, enabling BSB's accounting team to reconcile each independently.

BSB receives daily rewards accrual reports showing total points earned across the portfolio by product, agent bank, and card. Daily redemption reports break down points redeemed by channel including statement credit, DDA deposit, gift card, transaction-level, partner transfer, and charitable donation. Rewards liability reports show outstanding unredeemed points for balance sheet management. Settlement reconciliation files in machine-readable format map rewards activity to accounting entries for GL import. Agent bank-level reporting segments rewards activity by partner for per-partner P&L tracking. All reports are accessible through the Enterprise Workflow portal and can be exported in standard formats or delivered via automated SFTP feeds.

Because the rewards engine is natively built into the Brim platform rather than a third-party bolt-on, there is no reconciliation gap between the transaction processor and the rewards engine. Earn, redemption, and settlement are all managed within one integrated system.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER:
BSB asks about reconciliation mechanics (DDA vs. statement credit) and accounting tracking. Accounting treatment detail (contra-revenue entry) signals awareness of financial reporting implications. "No reconciliation gap" is the strategic message because a single integrated system avoids multi-vendor matching issues.

WHAT IS ASSUMED:
- DDA deposit requires integration with Jack Henry, scoped during implementation
- Automated reconciliation file delivery (SFTP) configured during implementation
- Rewards liability reporting formats aligned with BSB's accounting requirements

WHAT IS MISSING:
- Has Brim done a Jack Henry core banking integration before? Estimated effort?
- Can we provide BSB with sample reconciliation report formats during the RFP?
- Does Brim calculate the estimated liability dollar amount, or does BSB apply their own actuarial assumptions?

ACTION BEFORE APRIL 10: Get sample reconciliation report formats from Brim. Confirm JH integration feasibility.

- DDA deposit integration with Jack Henry: has Brim done a Jack Henry core banking integration before? Estimated level of effort?
- Can we provide BSB with sample reconciliation report formats during the RFP process?
- Rewards liability reporting: does Brim calculate the estimated liability dollar amount, or does BSB apply their own actuarial assumptions to the points balance data?
- What format are the settlement reconciliation files delivered in (CSV, fixed-width, XML)?
questions.pricing: Y - Included in base pricing. Core banking integration (e.g., Jack Henry for DDA deposits) may require additional implementation scoping.

questions.requirement: Describe how your solution integrates & synchronizes with loyalty services (redemption platforms like Mastercard Reward Solutions (MRS) and POS redemption solution). Describe your ability to integrate your rewards solution with BSB's mobile and digital OLB via SSO.
questions.bullet:
BRIM FINANCIAL supports integration and synchronization with external loyalty services, redemption platforms, and BSB's mobile and digital OLB via SSO.

________________________________________
INTEGRATION WITH LOYALTY SERVICES AND REDEMPTION PLATFORMS
________________________________________

- BRIM FINANCIAL operates its own proprietary rewards and loyalty platform as the primary engine, proven in market with partners including AFFINITY CREDIT UNION and Air France-KLM Flying Blue
- The API-first architecture (300+ endpoints) enables integration with third-party loyalty and redemption platforms including Mastercard Rewards Solutions (MRS)
- Integration options:
 - Real-time API integration for synchronizing earn/burn data with external platforms
 - Batch file integration (SFTP/secure API) for scheduled data exchange
 - Earn on Brim / Burn Elsewhere: Cardholders earn on the Brim platform and redeem on a connected partner platform (live in production today with Flying Blue)
 - Earn Elsewhere / Burn on Brim: Rewards earned through external programs can be imported into the Brim rewards ledger for redemption within the Brim ecosystem

________________________________________
POS REDEMPTION
________________________________________

- Brim's rewards model is digital-first: cardholders redeem points against posted transactions directly in the mobile app or online portal (swipe-to-redeem)
- No POS-level integration required at merchant locations
- Redemption is available at any merchant where the card is accepted, not limited to merchants with specific POS configurations
- If BSB requires POS-level redemption for specific use cases, the platform can support integration through its API framework (to be scoped during implementation)

________________________________________
SSO INTEGRATION WITH BSB's MOBILE AND DIGITAL OLB
________________________________________

- BRIM FINANCIAL supports Single Sign-On integration using industry-standard protocols (SAML 2.0, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect)
- Cardholders log into BSB's OLB or mobile app once and access credit card account, rewards dashboard, transaction history, and redemption directly
- The Brim-powered rewards portal is embedded within BSB's digital environment maintaining brand consistency
- This SSO capability extends to agent bank partners, with each able to embed the rewards experience within their own digital banking environment

________________________________________
LIVE EXAMPLE
________________________________________

- AFFINITY CREDIT UNION members access their credit card and rewards portal through their online banking environment, powered by BRIM FINANCIAL's SSO-integrated platform
questions.paragraph:
BRIM FINANCIAL supports integration and synchronization with external loyalty services, redemption platforms including Mastercard Rewards Solutions (MRS), and BSB's mobile and digital OLB via SSO.

BRIM FINANCIAL operates its own proprietary rewards and loyalty platform as the primary engine, proven in market with financial institution partners including AFFINITY CREDIT UNION and brand partners such as Air France-KLM Flying Blue. The API-first architecture with over 300 endpoints enables integration with third-party loyalty and redemption platforms including MRS. Integration options include real-time API synchronization, batch file exchange via SFTP, and bidirectional earn/burn models. The earn-on-Brim, burn-elsewhere model is live in production today with Flying Blue, and the reverse (earn elsewhere, burn on Brim) is also supported where rewards earned through external programs are imported into the Brim rewards ledger.

Brim's rewards model is digital-first. Cardholders redeem points against posted transactions directly in the mobile app or online portal with no POS-level integration required at merchant locations. This approach offers redemption at any merchant where the card is accepted rather than only at merchants with specific POS configurations, reducing operational complexity. If BSB requires POS-level redemption for specific use cases, the platform can support integration through its API framework.

SSO integration with BSB's existing mobile banking app and digital online banking platform uses industry-standard protocols including SAML 2.0, OAuth 2.0, and OpenID Connect. Cardholders log in once and access their credit card account, rewards dashboard, transaction history, and redemption directly within BSB's branded environment. This SSO capability extends to agent bank partners, with each able to embed the Brim-powered rewards experience within their own digital banking environment. AFFINITY CREDIT UNION members currently access their credit card and rewards portal through their online banking environment powered by BRIM FINANCIAL's SSO-integrated platform.
questions.rationale:

BSB asks about integration with loyalty services (names MRS and POS redemption) and SSO with BSB's OLB. MRS addressed directly. POS redemption reframed as digital-first being superior (available at any merchant vs. only POS-equipped ones). SSO given prominent treatment with specific protocol names for BSB's tech team.
