questions.paragraph: Fraud reporting covers two areas: application-level fraud and portfolio-level transaction fraud. On the application side, reports show flagged applications by type (synthetic identity, identity theft, bust-out attempt), source channel, and disposition. TruValidate hit reports detail identity verification failures, device risk signals, and synthetic identity flags. BSB can see fraud rates by channel — online, in-branch, and agent bank — and track application fraud trends over time. For transaction and portfolio fraud, reports cover gross and net fraud losses broken down by product, agent bank FI, MCC, geography, and time period. Fraud rate is shown as a percentage of total transactions and total dollar volume. Case management metrics include open case count, average resolution time, false positive rate, and recovery rate. Chargeback reporting shows volume, dollar amount, win/loss rate, and reason code distribution. Reports are available in the admin portal dashboard, exportable to CSV or PDF, and can be delivered via SFTP on a schedule BSB defines.

questions.requirement: Describe how the solution provides real-time fraud analysis on each authorization request to enable detection and prevention. Can you provide fraud decisioning latency ≤ 300 ms p95; outreach trigger creation ≤ 5 seconds?
questions.bullet:
Real-time fraud analysis runs on every authorization request. Fraud decisioning targets sub-300ms latency at p95. REAL-TIME ANALYSIS:
- Every authorization passes through the fraud rules engine before approval
- Three-tier analysis: Level 1 (auth-level rules), Level 2 (pre-approval deep analysis), Near Real-Time (pattern detection)
- 3,000+ data fields evaluated per transaction
- Fraud decisioning latency target: ≤ 300ms at p95 OUTREACH TRIGGERS:
- Fraud alerts trigger customer outreach within configurable thresholds (target: under 60 seconds for high-severity alerts)
- Outreach channels: SMS, push notification, email, outbound IVR call
- Customer can confirm or deny the transaction via 2-way SMS or push notification
- If customer confirms fraud, the card is blocked immediately and a case is created DETECTION AND PREVENTION:
- Real-time blocking: Suspicious card can be blocked mid-authorization if fraud score exceeds threshold
- Velocity detection: Rapid transaction patterns flagged in real time
- Geographic anomaly detection: Transactions from unexpected locations flagged
- Device fingerprinting via TruValidate adds an identity risk signal to every digital transaction
questions.paragraph: Every authorization request passes through real-time fraud analysis before an approve or decline decision is returned. The fraud rules engine evaluates 3,000+ data fields per transaction across three monitoring tiers. Fraud decisioning targets sub-300ms latency at the 95th percentile, keeping the analysis invisible to the cardholder's transaction experience. When fraud is suspected, the system triggers customer outreach through the configured channel — SMS, push notification, email, or outbound IVR call. The target is outreach within 60 seconds for high-severity alerts. Cardholders can confirm or deny the transaction via two-way SMS or push notification. If the customer confirms fraud, the card is blocked immediately and a fraud case is created for analyst review. The system detects fraud patterns in real time: rapid velocity spikes, geographic anomalies, device fingerprint mismatches (via TruValidate), and threshold breaches on custom rules. A suspicious card can be blocked mid-authorization if the fraud score exceeds the configured threshold.

- Changes take effect in real time — no system restart or batch cycle

questions.paragraph: The system ships with pre-built fraud detection rules covering high-risk MCCs, velocity anomalies, geographic inconsistencies, known fraud patterns, large transaction thresholds, and card-not-present risk indicators. These baseline rules are informed by patterns observed across Brim's existing portfolio. BSB can create custom rules using a point-and-click rule builder. The rules engine supports 3,000+ data fields with AND/OR/NOT logic, threshold comparisons, time-window aggregations, and pattern matching. Before deploying a custom rule to production, BSB can test it against historical transaction data in the sandbox to evaluate its effectiveness and false positive rate. Rules can be added, modified, or disabled at any time through the admin interface. Changes take effect in real time — no system restart, no batch cycle. Every rule change is logged with the user who made it, what changed, when, and the stated reason.

questions.requirement: Describe the ability for BSB to override fraud flags and allow transactions to go through.
questions.bullet:
Yes. Authorized BSB staff can override fraud flags and allow flagged transactions to proceed. - Override authority: Configurable by role — only designated Fraud Analysts and Program Administrators can override
- Override process: Analyst reviews the flagged transaction, documents the reason for override, and releases the transaction
- The override is logged: who overrode, when, the fraud score at the time, and the stated reason
- Overrides can be applied to: individual transactions, temporary card-level holds, and account-level blocks
- Real-time: Once overridden, the transaction proceeds on the next attempt (if card was blocked, card is unblocked)
- BSB can set limits on override authority (e.g., max transaction amount that can be overridden without supervisor approval)
questions.paragraph: Yes. Authorized BSB staff can override fraud flags and allow transactions to proceed. Override authority is role-based — only designated Fraud Analysts and Program Administrators can do it. BSB configures which roles have override permission and can set limits, such as a maximum transaction amount that requires supervisor approval for override. The override process requires the analyst to review the flagged transaction and document a reason before releasing it. The system logs the override with the analyst's identity, timestamp, the fraud score at the time of the flag, and the stated reason. Overrides can be applied to individual transactions, temporary card-level holds, or account-level blocks. Once overridden, the card is unblocked and the next transaction attempt proceeds normally.

questions.requirement: Describe how your transactional fraud monitoring eliminates the need for customers to enter travel notes in advance of travel.
questions.bullet:
Brim's fraud monitoring system uses behavioral analysis and pattern recognition, eliminating the need for travel notes. - The system builds a spending profile for each cardholder over time: typical transaction locations, MCCs, amounts, frequency, and time-of-day patterns
- When a transaction occurs in an unusual location, it is scored against the cardholder's profile — not flagged solely because it is out-of-state or international
- Factors considered: Is the cardholder's device present? Have they transacted in this region before? Does the transaction fit their spending pattern?
- Device fingerprinting via TruValidate adds location and device signals
- If the transaction is borderline, 2-way notification asks the cardholder to confirm rather than blocking outright
- No travel notes needed. Cardholders do not need to inform BSB before traveling
- This reduces false declines that frustrate customers while maintaining fraud protection
questions.paragraph: Brim's fraud monitoring builds a behavioral spending profile for each cardholder: typical transaction locations, merchant categories, amounts, frequency, and time-of-day patterns. When a transaction occurs in an unusual location, the system scores it against this profile rather than applying a blanket geographic block. The scoring considers multiple signals: Is the cardholder's device present (via TruValidate device fingerprinting)? Have they transacted in this region before? Does the transaction amount and MCC fit their typical pattern? A cardholder who regularly buys airline tickets and hotel stays will not be flagged for a hotel charge in a new city. If a transaction is borderline, the system sends a two-way notification asking the cardholder to confirm rather than declining outright. This eliminates the need for travel notes. Cardholders do not need to call BSB or log into the app to register upcoming trips. The result is fewer false declines — which is the primary driver of customer dissatisfaction in credit card programs — while maintaining effective fraud detection.

questions.bullet:
Fraud outreach strategies are fully customizable by channel, trigger, and customer segment. OUTREACH CHANNELS:
- SMS (2-way): Cardholder receives text, replies to confirm/deny
- Push notification: In-app alert with confirm/deny action buttons
- Email: For lower-priority alerts or follow-up communications

- Opt-in management: Customers opt in to electronic/text communications during onboarding or through self-service portal
- Real-time alerts: 150+ notification types available; BSB selects which events trigger customer alerts
- Message templates: Customizable text and branding per agent bank FI
- Escalation: If customer does not respond within a configurable window, the system escalates to the next channel or creates a case for agent follow-up
questions.paragraph: Fraud outreach strategies are customizable by channel, trigger, and customer segment. BSB defines which fraud events trigger outreach: specific fraud score thresholds, rule hits, transaction patterns, or manual agent escalation. Available channels include two-way SMS (cardholder receives a text and replies to confirm or deny), push notifications (in-app alert with action buttons), email (for lower-priority alerts), and outbound IVR calls (for high-severity alerts or customers without digital channels). BSB sets the channel priority per customer segment — for example, push notification first, then SMS if no response within two minutes, then outbound call. Customers opt in to electronic and text communications during onboarding or through the self-service portal. Message templates are customizable with per-FI branding. If a customer does not respond within a configurable window, the system escalates to the next channel or creates a case for agent follow-up. Brim supports 150+ notification types; BSB selects which events trigger customer-facing alerts.

questions.paragraph:
The platform provides two-way fraud notifications across SMS, push notifications, and outbound IVR calls — live in production.

When the fraud engine flags a suspicious transaction, the cardholder receives a notification through the configured channel. SMS alerts describe the transaction; the customer replies YES (legitimate) or NO (fraud), triggering automatic case action. Push notifications present in-app Confirm/Deny buttons with instant response. Outbound IVR calls use keypad prompts for high-severity alerts. When a cardholder calls in, the agent sees the alert, transaction details, and recommended action in the workspace.

For point-of-sale confirmation, real-time alerts notify the cardholder immediately after a suspicious transaction. If the card is soft-blocked pending confirmation, the cardholder confirms via push or SMS before the next transaction proceeds. Fraud context passes to BSB's Glia deployment via API, so Glia-powered sessions include alert details and account context.

Brim's fraud notification system supports 150+ notification types. The 2-way SMS and push flows are live across the production portfolio, including Zolve/Continental Bank in the US where real-time fraud alerting runs on the Mastercard network.

questions.requirement: Describe your ability to integrate with Verafin to support fraud detection in applications and existing accounts. Can you provide a feed/API to deliver transactional data into Verafin for AML?
questions.bullet:
Yes. Brim can deliver transaction data to BSB's Verafin deployment for AML monitoring. This creates a double layer of AML coverage. VERAFIN INTEGRATION:
- Daily transaction data feed: Brim generates a file containing all credit card transaction data (authorizations, postings, payments, adjustments) and delivers it to Verafin via SFTP
- File format: Configurable to match Verafin's ingestion requirements
- Application data: New account application data (applicant details, decision, source channel) can also be included in the feed for fraud-in-applications monitoring
- Existing accounts: Ongoing transaction activity flows to Verafin daily so BSB's AML team has full visibility into credit card behavior DOUBLE-LAYER AML:
- Layer 1 (Brim): Brim runs its own AML screening through Refinitiv World-Check One at application and periodically during the account lifecycle. PEP, sanctions, and adverse media checks
- Layer 2 (BSB/Verafin): BSB's Verafin deployment receives Brim's transaction data and applies BSB's own AML rules and suspicious activity detection models - Brim and Verafin are complementary — Brim screens at the platform level, Verafin screens at the bank level
- BSB's existing Verafin workflows, alert handling, and SAR filing processes remain unchanged
questions.paragraph: Brim can deliver transaction data to BSB's Verafin deployment for AML monitoring. The integration uses a daily SFTP file containing all credit card transaction data — authorizations, postings, payments, and adjustments — formatted to match Verafin's ingestion requirements. Application data (applicant details, decision outcome, source channel) can also be included so Verafin has visibility into fraud-in-applications patterns. This creates a double layer of AML coverage. At the platform level, Brim runs its own screening through Refinitiv World-Check One. Every applicant is checked against PEP lists, sanctions lists, and adverse media at application and periodically during the account lifecycle. At the bank level, BSB's Verafin deployment receives the credit card transaction data and applies BSB's own AML rules and suspicious activity detection models. The two systems are complementary. Brim catches issues at the platform level before they reach BSB. Verafin gives BSB's AML team the ability to analyze credit card activity alongside deposit and other banking activity for a consolidated view. BSB's existing Verafin workflows, alert handling, and SAR filing processes remain unchanged — Brim simply adds a new data feed into the existing infrastructure.

questions.requirement: Describe how your system supports 3D-S. Please indicate which versions your systems support. Describe how you will maintain the system at the most current 3-D secure 2.x. Please describe the impact to the issuer during this upgrade (please use an example of where a current issuer client has moved from 3D-S 2.1 to 2.2). Describe your ability to put an additional security layer for online transaction (e.g., 3D-S controls for a non 3D-S transaction). Describe your capabilities to integrate with a specific vendor that provides an access control server to provide 3DS data.
questions.bullet:
Brim supports 3D Secure 2.x (EMV 3DS) for online transaction authentication through the Mastercard network. 3D SECURE CAPABILITIES:
- Version: 3DS 2.x (current EMV 3-D Secure specification). 3DS 1.0 is deprecated per Mastercard mandate
- Network: Mastercard Identity Check (Mastercard's implementation of 3DS 2.x)
- Frictionless flow: Low-risk transactions are authenticated without cardholder interaction using risk-based analysis
- Challenge flow: Higher-risk transactions prompt cardholder verification (biometric, OTP, or knowledge-based)
- Data sharing: 3DS 2.x passes 150+ data elements between merchant and issuer for better risk assessment
- Version maintenance: Brim updates 3DS support in line with Mastercard mandates and EMV specification updates
- Liability shift: 3DS-authenticated transactions shift fraud liability from BSB to the merchant/acquirer
- Impact to customers: Frictionless flow means most online transactions proceed without additional steps. Challenge flow adds one verification step for flagged transactions
questions.paragraph: Brim supports 3D Secure 2.x (the current EMV 3-D Secure specification) through Mastercard Identity Check. 3DS 1.0 is deprecated per Mastercard mandate and is not used. 3DS 2.x operates in two flows. The frictionless flow authenticates low-risk transactions without cardholder interaction — risk-based analysis using 150+ data elements passed between the merchant and issuer determines that the transaction is legitimate, and the cardholder proceeds without an additional step. The challenge flow prompts higher-risk transactions for cardholder verification through biometric, one-time password, or knowledge-based authentication. For BSB's customers, the practical impact is minimal. Most online transactions go through the frictionless flow. When a challenge is required, the cardholder completes one additional verification step. Fraud liability on 3DS-authenticated transactions shifts from BSB to the merchant/acquirer. Brim maintains 3DS support in line with Mastercard's mandates and EMV specification updates. As new versions are released, Brim updates accordingly so BSB stays current without managing the certification process.

questions.requirement: Describe how you monitor for and report unusual or suspicious activity on individual accounts or across the full portfolio (e.g., if BSB begins seeing a slew of transactions starting for a particular vendor that have not occurred in our portfolio in the last 30 days).
questions.bullet:
Monitoring for unusual or suspicious activity operates at both the individual account and portfolio level. INDIVIDUAL ACCOUNT MONITORING:
- Behavioral profiling: Each account builds a spending profile over time. Deviations trigger alerts
- Velocity monitoring: Rapid transaction patterns flagged in real time
- Amount anomalies: Transactions significantly above the cardholder's typical spend pattern
- Geographic anomalies: Transactions from unexpected locations
- Device anomalies: New or suspicious devices via TruValidate PORTFOLIO-LEVEL MONITORING:
- Cross-portfolio pattern detection: Near Real-Time tier identifies patterns across multiple accounts (e.g., multiple cards used at the same compromised merchant)
- MCC concentration: Spike in transactions at a specific merchant category across the portfolio
- Geographic concentration: Unusual transaction volume from a specific region
- Common-point-of-purchase (CPP): Identifies merchant locations linked to multiple fraud cases
- BSB receives alerts and can drill into affected accounts REPORTING:
- Suspicious activity dashboard with trend lines, heat maps, and drill-down capability
- Automated reports on portfolio-level anomalies delivered on configurable schedule
- Data available to feed into BSB's Verafin for SAR analysis
questions.paragraph: Monitoring operates at both the individual account and portfolio level. At the account level, the system builds a behavioral spending profile for each cardholder and flags deviations: velocity spikes, amount anomalies, geographic inconsistencies, and new or suspicious devices (via TruValidate device fingerprinting). At the portfolio level, the Near Real-Time monitoring tier detects patterns across multiple accounts. If BSB starts seeing a concentration of transactions at a specific MCC, a spike in fraud from a particular region, or multiple cards showing up at the same compromised merchant, the system flags it. Common-point-of-purchase analysis identifies merchant locations linked to multiple fraud cases so BSB can take proactive action — blocking the merchant or alerting affected cardholders. BSB receives portfolio-level alerts and can drill into affected accounts from the suspicious activity dashboard. Trend lines, heat maps, and drill-down capability support investigation. Automated reports on portfolio anomalies can be delivered on a configurable schedule. This data also feeds into BSB's Verafin deployment for SAR analysis and regulatory reporting.

questions.requirement: Describe what solutions you provide for rewards fraud prevention and detection .
questions.bullet:
Brim monitors rewards activity for fraud patterns including point manipulation, abuse, and synthetic redemptions. REWARDS FRAUD DETECTION:
- Velocity rules on redemptions: Flags rapid or high-volume redemption activity
- Points balance anomalies: Alerts on unusual point accumulation patterns (e.g., earn-and-return schemes)
- Redemption channel monitoring: Tracks redemption source (online, phone, agent) for anomalies
- Account linking: Detects if multiple accounts are being used to pool or transfer points fraudulently
- Return abuse detection: Flags patterns of purchase-earn-return that accumulate points without real spend
- Merchant collusion indicators: Unusual earn patterns at specific merchants PREVENTION:
- Redemption holds: Configurable hold period on newly earned points before they are redeemable
- Redemption limits: Daily/weekly/monthly caps on point redemption
- Approval workflows: High-value redemptions can require agent approval
- Account-level controls: Individual accounts can be flagged for enhanced rewards monitoring This capability runs in production across all Brim programs — processing transactions for Affinity CU, Manulife, UNI, Zolve, and Continental Currency Exchange with 99.97% uptime over the past 12 months.
questions.paragraph: Brim monitors rewards activity for fraud patterns. The system applies velocity rules to redemptions, flagging rapid or high-volume redemption activity. Points balance anomalies — such as unusual accumulation patterns from earn-and-return schemes — trigger alerts. Redemption channel monitoring tracks whether redemptions come from online, phone, or agent channels and flags deviations. The system also detects multi-account point pooling, return abuse patterns (purchase, earn, return to keep the points), and merchant collusion indicators where unusual earn patterns concentrate at specific merchants. Prevention controls include configurable hold periods on newly earned points before they become redeemable, daily/weekly/monthly redemption caps, and approval workflows that require agent sign-off on high-value redemptions. Individual accounts can be flagged for enhanced rewards monitoring if suspicious patterns emerge.

questions.requirement: Describe how you support monitoring and prevention of credit abuse.
questions.bullet:
Credit abuse monitoring uses behavioral analytics and configurable rules to detect and prevent misuse. CREDIT ABUSE DETECTION:
- Bust-out detection: Flags accounts showing the classic bust-out pattern — rapid credit line usage approaching limit, minimal payments, then default
- Over-limit behavior: Monitors accounts consistently operating near or at credit limit
- Payment pattern anomalies: Detects payment-then-spend cycling (pay down, spend up, repeat) that indicates potential bust-out
- Cash advance abuse: Flags excessive cash advance usage relative to total spend
- Balance transfer cycling: Detects repeated balance transfers used to maintain high utilization across products
- Authorized user abuse: Monitors authorized users for spend patterns inconsistent with the primary cardholder PREVENTION:
- Automated credit line reduction for high-risk accounts
- Account review triggers routed to credit analyst queue
- Proactive account restrictions (e.g., block cash advances, block balance transfers) applied without closing the account
- BSB defines the thresholds and actions per risk tier
questions.paragraph: Brim monitors for credit abuse using behavioral analytics and configurable rules. Bust-out detection flags accounts showing the classic pattern: rapid credit utilization approaching the limit, minimal payments, then sudden default. The system watches for payment-then-spend cycling, excessive cash advance usage, balance transfer cycling across products, and authorized user spend patterns inconsistent with the primary cardholder. Prevention tools include automated credit line reduction for accounts that hit risk thresholds, account review triggers routed to the credit analyst queue, and proactive account restrictions — blocking cash advances or balance transfers without closing the account. BSB defines the thresholds and corresponding actions per risk tier. Accounts flagged for credit abuse can be escalated for manual review or handled automatically based on the configured rules.

questions.requirement: Describe your fraud on-behalf services, including where these services start and end, and any areas not covered by your offering. Provide details on the BSB involvement in these processes and how fraud on-behalf servicing is handed over to BSB internal fraud teams. Outline all relevant processes and procedures.
questions.bullet:
Brim provides fraud on-behalf services covering real-time monitoring through case resolution. BSB retains oversight and escalation authority. WHAT BRIM HANDLES (ON-BEHALF):
- Real-time fraud monitoring: 3-tier analysis on every authorization
- Alert generation: Fraud alerts created automatically from rules engine hits
- Customer outreach: 2-way SMS, push, and IVR notifications to cardholders on suspected fraud
- Card blocking: Immediate card block on confirmed fraud
- Case creation and initial triage: Cases auto-created with severity, transaction details, and customer contact history
- First-level investigation: Brim fraud analysts investigate and resolve standard fraud cases
- Dispute filing: Mastercard chargeback initiation for confirmed fraud transactions WHERE BSB TAKES OVER:
- Complex or high-dollar cases: Escalated to BSB's internal fraud team based on configurable thresholds
- SAR filing: BSB handles all SAR filing through Verafin using data from Brim's fraud cases and transaction feeds
- Policy decisions: BSB approves changes to fraud rules, thresholds, and outreach strategies
- Portfolio-level fraud strategy: BSB sets overall risk appetite and fraud loss targets HANDOVER PROCESS:
- Cases escalated to BSB include full investigation notes, transaction evidence, customer contact log, and recommended action
- BSB fraud team accesses the case in the shared case management platform
- Escalation criteria are configurable — BSB defines what triggers escalation (dollar amount, case type, customer tier, etc.)
- Regular fraud review meetings between Brim and BSB fraud teams to review trends and adjust strategies
questions.paragraph: Brim provides fraud on-behalf services covering the full cycle from real-time monitoring through case resolution, with clear boundaries for BSB involvement. Brim handles real-time fraud monitoring (three-tier analysis on every authorization), alert generation, customer outreach via two-way SMS/push/IVR, immediate card blocking on confirmed fraud, case creation and initial triage, first-level investigation by Brim fraud analysts, and Mastercard chargeback initiation for confirmed fraud transactions. BSB takes over on complex or high-dollar cases that exceed configurable thresholds. When a case is escalated, BSB's fraud team receives the full investigation file: notes, transaction evidence, customer contact log, and recommended action. BSB also handles all SAR filing through Verafin using data from Brim's fraud cases and the daily transaction feed. Policy decisions — fraud rules, thresholds, outreach strategies — require BSB approval. Escalation criteria are configurable. BSB defines what triggers handover: dollar amount, case type, customer tier, or specific fraud patterns. Regular fraud review meetings between Brim and BSB teams cover emerging trends, rule effectiveness, and strategy adjustments. Areas not covered by Brim's on-behalf services: SAR filing (BSB via Verafin), law enforcement liaison (BSB), and portfolio-level fraud strategy decisions (BSB sets risk appetite and loss targets).

questions.requirement: Describe how you can support payments posting 24 hours a day and have a payment cutoff happen automatically.
questions.bullet:
Payments post 24 hours a day with automated cutoff processing. - Payment methods: ACH, online portal, mobile app, phone (IVR and agent-assisted), in-branch, wire transfer, and check
- 24/7 posting: Payments received at any time are posted to the account. No batch windows that prevent posting
- Automated cutoff: Configurable cutoff time (e.g., 5:00 PM ET). Payments received before cutoff are credited same-day. Payments after cutoff are credited next business day
- Cutoff time is configurable by BSB and can differ by payment method
- Weekend/holiday processing: Payments received on weekends or holidays are posted per BSB's configured policy (immediate or next business day)
- Real-time OTB update: Payment posting immediately increases available credit
questions.paragraph: Payments post 24 hours a day. The system accepts payments via ACH, online portal, mobile app, phone (IVR and agent-assisted), in-branch, wire transfer, and check. There are no batch windows that block payment posting. BSB configures an automated cutoff time — for example, 5:00 PM ET. Payments received before cutoff are credited same-day. Payments received after cutoff are credited the next business day. The cutoff time can differ by payment method if BSB requires. Weekend and holiday payment processing follows BSB's configured policy — either immediate posting or next business day crediting. When a payment posts, the cardholder's available credit increases immediately. The payment is reflected in the cardholder portal and mobile app in real time.

Please describe whether your system supports the processing of all payment network's (Mastercard/Visa/Discover/Amex) and on-us transaction files and provide all of the information necessary to support settlement and reconciliation as follows:

questions.bullet: Brim's platform is currently certified and live on the Mastercard network. For BSB's multi-network requirements (Visa, Discover, Amex), Brim's network-agnostic architecture supports certification with additional networks. The implementation roadmap includes Visa certification as a priority deliverable. Brim's processing engine is designed to handle multi-network transaction routing, settlement, and reporting. BSB's primary card program can launch on Mastercard while additional network certifications are completed in parallel.
questions.paragraph:
Brim processes on the Mastercard network through TSYS — the only card network currently supported. Visa, Discover, and Amex are not available today.

For Mastercard, Brim handles full clearing file processing: incoming presentments, outgoing chargebacks, representments, and adjustments. Daily settlement runs with automated reconciliation between TSYS settlement data, Mastercard clearing data, and Brim's internal records. Discrepancies are flagged with automated retry. On-us transactions settle internally. Settlement data is delivered daily with transaction-level detail, GL entries mapped to BSB's chart of accounts, chargeback tracking, and fee/interchange reporting.

Mastercard-only is a practical fit for BSB's program. Mastercard has universal merchant acceptance in the US, and Brim is Mastercard's selected fintech partner for US credit card platform launches. BSB benefits from this strategic relationship: joint go-to-market support for the agent bank program, access to Mastercard innovation programs, dedicated relationship support, and pricing advantages. BSB's agent bank FIs issue under BSB's BIN on the Mastercard network — a proven model.

If BSB has an existing Visa portfolio that needs migration, that requires separate network-level planning and a multi-month TSYS integration. Adding Discover or Amex follows the same path.

questions.requirement: Describe your ability to support product parameter configurability and compliance for client FIs (e.g., supporting a credit union client in setting a maximum APR of 18%, etc.). What parameters can be set (e.g., fee caps, interest rate caps, fee posting timeline, etc.)? How do you support management of and customer communication surrounding parameter maintenance?
questions.bullet:
Product parameters are configurable through the Brim admin portal per product and per agent bank FI. BSB controls compliance boundaries. CONFIGURABLE PARAMETERS:
- Interest rates: Separate APRs for purchases, cash advances, balance transfers, and promotional rates. Risk-based pricing supported. Variable rates tied to prime
- Fees: Annual fees, late fees, over-limit fees, cash advance fees, balance transfer fees, foreign transaction fees — all configurable per product
- Credit limits: Minimum and maximum limits per product. Individual adjustments supported
- APR caps: BSB or agent bank FI can set a maximum APR (e.g., credit union client caps at 18%)
- Grace periods: Configurable per transaction type
- Minimum payments: Configurable calculation method and minimum dollar floor
- Penalty rates: Configurable trigger conditions and penalty APR COMPLIANCE CONTROLS:
- Parameters can be locked at the program level by BSB so agent bank FIs cannot exceed defined boundaries
- Example: BSB sets max APR at 29.99%, a credit union FI sets its max at 18% within that boundary
- All parameter changes require approval workflow and generate audit entries
questions.paragraph: Product parameters are configurable through the Brim admin portal at the product level and at the agent bank FI level. BSB can set program-wide boundaries, and each agent bank FI can operate within those boundaries based on its own policies and regulations. Configurable parameters include: APRs for purchases, cash advances, balance transfers, and promotional rates (risk-based pricing and variable rates tied to prime are supported); fees including annual, late, over-limit, cash advance, balance transfer, and foreign transaction fees; credit limit ranges; grace periods per transaction type; minimum payment calculation methods; and penalty rate triggers and APRs. For compliance, BSB can lock parameters at the program level. If BSB sets the program-wide maximum APR at 29.99%, a credit union agent bank can set its own max at 18% — but cannot exceed BSB's ceiling. This ensures every FI in the program operates within BSB's compliance framework while having flexibility to set their own competitive positioning. All parameter changes go through an approval workflow and generate audit entries.

questions.bullet:
Billing cycles are configurable per product, segment, and hierarchy level. Multiple cycle periods are supported on the same product. - Cycle periods: Monthly is standard. Other periods (e.g., 25-day, 28-day) are configurable
- Per-product cycles: Different products can have different billing cycles
- Per-segment cycles: Within a product, different customer segments can have different cycle dates
- Per-FI cycles: Each agent bank FI can have its own billing cycle configuration
- Multiple cycles on same product: Supported. BSB can stagger statement dates across the portfolio to distribute processing and payment volume
- Cycle date assignment: New accounts assigned to a cycle based on configurable rules (e.g., application date, geographic region, or round-robin)
- Cycle changes: Account-level cycle date changes supported with configurable effective date
questions.paragraph: Billing cycles are configurable at the product, segment, and FI level. Monthly is the standard period, but other periods (25-day, 28-day) are also configurable. Different products can run different cycles. Within a product, BSB can assign different customer segments to different cycle dates. Multiple billing cycles on the same product are supported. BSB can stagger statement dates across the portfolio — for example, four cycle dates per month — to distribute payment processing volume evenly. New accounts are assigned to a cycle based on configurable rules: application date, geographic region, or round-robin assignment. Each agent bank FI can have its own billing cycle configuration. Cycle date changes at the individual account level are supported with a configurable effective date.

questions.requirement: Describe how you support different APRs for Balance Transfers, Cash Advances, and Purchases. Describe how you support parameterized interest rates based on the number of balance transfers, transactions, or ticket size of transaction. Describe how balance transfers, cash advances, and installments can be pulled into a separate section of a statement. Describe how you add in protected balance at lower APR. How do you define APRs and display APR to the cardholder (statement, online, etc.)?
questions.bullet:
Different APRs for balance transfers, cash advances, and purchases are standard. Interest calculation supports parameterized rates based on product design. APR STRUCTURE:
- Separate APRs: Purchases, cash advances, balance transfers, and promotional rates each carry their own APR
- Variable rates: Tied to prime rate or other index, with configurable margin
- Risk-based pricing: APR assigned based on credit score band at origination or re-pricing review
- Promotional rates: 0% or reduced APR for configurable periods on balance transfers, purchases, or both
- Protected balances: Balances at a lower APR (e.g., promotional rate) are tracked separately and maintained until paid off PARAMETERIZED RATES:
- Number of balance transfers: Rate can differ based on volume (e.g., first BT at 0%, subsequent at standard)
- Transaction type: Each type carries its own rate
- Balance tiers: APR can vary based on balance size
- Interest calculation: Daily periodic rate applied to average daily balance
- Minimum interest charge: Configurable floor
questions.paragraph: The platform supports separate APRs for purchases, cash advances, balance transfers, and promotional rates. Each carries its own rate, and interest is calculated independently on each balance bucket using the daily periodic rate method applied to the average daily balance. Variable rates are supported, tied to the prime rate or another index with a configurable margin. Risk-based pricing assigns the APR based on the applicant's credit score band at origination, and re-pricing reviews can adjust the rate during the account lifecycle. Promotional rates — 0% or reduced APR for a configurable period — can apply to balance transfers, purchases, or both. Protected balances at a lower rate are tracked separately and maintained until paid off per CARD Act requirements. Parameterized rates allow BSB to vary the APR based on the number of balance transfers (e.g., first BT at 0%, subsequent at the standard rate), balance tiers, or transaction type. A minimum interest charge floor is also configurable.

questions.requirement:
Describe your balance transfer process including:
 - Ability to provide an electric balance transfer rather than a check
 - Support a phone request for balance transfer
 - Ability to pre-populate the Balance Transfer disclosure and send it to the CRM
questions.bullet:
Balance transfers are electronic by default. The platform supports phone-initiated and pre-populated BT offers. BALANCE TRANSFER CAPABILITIES:
- Electronic balance transfers: Payment sent to the receiving institution electronically — no physical checks mailed
- Phone request: Cardholders or agents can initiate BT via phone (IVR or agent-assisted). The transaction is processed electronically
- Pre-populated BT forms: Known creditor information from credit bureau data can pre-populate the BT request form for the cardholder
- Online/mobile self-service: Cardholders initiate BTs through the portal or mobile app
- BT promotional rates: Configurable promotional APR and duration per product
- BT limits: Configurable per product (e.g., max BT amount, max number of BTs per cycle)
- BT fee: Configurable as flat amount or percentage of transfer
- Processing time: BT typically processes within 7-10 business days
- Status tracking: Cardholder and agent can track BT status in the portal
questions.paragraph: Balance transfers are electronic. No physical checks are mailed. The receiving institution gets an electronic payment, and the cardholder sees the balance transfer posted to their account. Cardholders can initiate balance transfers through the online portal, mobile app, or by phone (IVR or agent-assisted). Pre-populated BT request forms use known creditor information from credit bureau data, so the cardholder does not have to look up account numbers for their existing debts. BSB configures BT parameters per product: promotional APR and duration, maximum transfer amount, maximum number of transfers per cycle, and BT fee (flat amount or percentage). Processing typically takes 7-10 business days. Both the cardholder and servicing agents can track BT status through the portal.

questions.requirement: Please describe the functionality you provide around penalty fees. Describe the differences for consumer and business accounts. Please describe the standard penalty fee parameters you provide for balance repayment, which parameters can be customized, and at which level of the hierarchy fees can be set. Via which channels can penalty fee messages be sent?
questions.bullet:
Penalty fees are configurable per product with separate parameters for consumer and business accounts. PENALTY FEE TYPES:
- Late payment fee: Triggered when minimum payment is not received by the due date. Configurable amount (flat or tiered based on balance)
- Over-limit fee: Triggered when balance exceeds credit limit (if BSB opts to allow over-limit transactions). Configurable amount
- Returned payment fee: Triggered on ACH return or bounced payment. Configurable amount
- Cash advance fee: Flat amount or percentage of the transaction, whichever is greater CONSUMER VS. BUSINESS:
- Fee parameters are set per product — consumer and business products can have different fee structures
- Consumer fees: Subject to CARD Act limits (e.g., late fee reasonableness requirements)
- Business fees: May have different fee amounts and trigger rules based on BSB's commercial card policy
- Penalty APR: Configurable penalty rate triggered by delinquency (e.g., 60+ days past due). Consumer penalty APR subject to CARD Act review requirements CONFIGURATION:
- All penalty fees configurable through the admin interface
- Fee waivers available on a per-account basis with reason code and audit log
- Fee schedules can be changed with configurable effective dates
questions.paragraph: Penalty fees are configurable per product. Consumer and business products carry separate fee structures. For consumer accounts, late fees are triggered when the minimum payment is not received by the due date. The fee amount is configurable — flat or tiered based on balance — and subject to CARD Act reasonableness requirements. Over-limit fees apply if BSB opts to allow over-limit transactions. Returned payment fees trigger on ACH returns or bounced payments. Cash advance fees are set as a flat amount or percentage, whichever is greater. Business accounts can have different fee amounts and trigger rules based on BSB's commercial card policy. CARD Act restrictions may not apply to business accounts, giving BSB more flexibility in fee design. Penalty APR is configurable and triggered by delinquency thresholds — for example, 60+ days past due. For consumer accounts, CARD Act review requirements apply. Fee waivers are available on a per-account basis with reason code documentation and audit logging. Fee schedules can be updated with configurable effective dates.

questions.requirement: Describe which type of card fees are supported? Can personalization fees be applied to cards for custom cards or logos? Describe your ability to apply fees for different types of cards (e.g., metal, biometric, contactless, etc.) as well as for card quality. Please describe any limitations, if applicable.
questions.bullet:
The platform supports standard card fees and personalization fees for custom and premium card products. SUPPORTED FEE TYPES:
- Annual fee / membership fee
- Monthly maintenance fee
- Supplementary card fee (for authorized users)
- Card replacement fee
- Expedited delivery fee
- Foreign transaction fee
- Balance inquiry fee
- Statement copy fee
- Personalization fee: Applied for custom cards, special logos, or premium card materials CARD TYPE FEES:
- Standard plastic: Baseline fee (or no fee)
- Metal cards: Higher personalization/annual fee configurable
- Custom design cards: Fee applied for personalized card art or co-branded designs
- Virtual cards: Fee can be waived or set separately CONFIGURATION:
- Each fee is configurable per product and per FI
- Fees can be one-time, annual, monthly, or per-event
- Fee waivers: First-year waiver, spend-based waiver (e.g., waive annual fee if spend exceeds $X), or manual waiver with reason code This capability runs in production across all Brim programs — processing transactions for Affinity CU, Manulife, UNI, Zolve, and Continental Currency Exchange with 99.97% uptime over the past 12 months.
questions.paragraph: The platform supports a full range of card fees: annual/membership fees, monthly maintenance fees, supplementary card fees for authorized users, replacement fees, expedited delivery fees, foreign transaction fees, balance inquiry fees, and statement copy fees. Personalization fees can be applied for custom card designs, special logos, or premium materials. Fees differ by card type. Metal cards carry a higher annual or personalization fee. Custom design cards with personalized art or co-branded designs carry a separate design fee. Virtual cards can have their own fee schedule or be fee-free. Each fee is configurable per product and per agent bank FI. Fees can be structured as one-time, annual, monthly, or per-event. Waiver logic is configurable: first-year waiver, spend-based waiver (e.g., annual fee waived if annual spend exceeds a threshold), or manual waiver with a reason code and audit entry.

questions.requirement: Please describe all possible triggers to start charging a periodical fee according to the following options (e.g., immediately, after N months, after card activation, after first successful authorization, after first movement, N months after card activation, and inactivity).
questions.bullet:
Periodic fee triggers are configurable. Multiple trigger options are available. FEE TRIGGER OPTIONS:
- Immediately at account creation: Fee charged on day one
- After N months: Fee deferred for a configurable number of months (e.g., first annual fee charged after month 12)
- After card activation: Fee charged only when the card is first activated
- After first successful authorization: Fee charged when the first transaction posts
- On anniversary date: Annual fee charged on the account anniversary, annually recurring
- On statement date: Fee charged on each billing cycle close CONFIGURATION:
- Each fee type (annual, monthly, supplementary card, etc.) can have its own trigger
- Trigger is set per product — different products can have different trigger logic for the same fee type
- BSB can combine triggers with waiver rules (e.g., charge annual fee after 12 months but waive if spend exceeds $3,000)
questions.paragraph: Periodic fee charging supports multiple trigger options: immediately at account creation, after a configurable number of months, after card activation, after the first successful authorization, on the account anniversary date, or on the statement cycle date. Each fee type can have its own trigger. An annual fee might be triggered on the account anniversary date. A supplementary card fee might be charged immediately when the authorized user card is issued. A monthly maintenance fee might start after card activation. Triggers are set per product, so different products can use different trigger logic for the same fee type. BSB can combine triggers with waiver rules — for example, charge the annual fee after 12 months but waive it if the cardholder's annual spend exceeds a defined threshold.

questions.requirement: Is it possible to set up different fees and/or waive the fee for certain defined periods for any card? Please describe what kind of fees are possible to define by time periods, if applicable. Is it possible to automatically prorate annual fees in the system?
questions.bullet:
Yes. Fees can be set for defined time periods and waived for specific periods. TIME-BASED FEE CONFIGURATION:
- First-year fee waiver: Annual fee waived for the first year, then charged on anniversary
- Introductory period: Reduced or zero fee for a configurable introductory period (e.g., first 6 months)
- Promotional waiver: Fee waived for a defined period tied to a campaign (e.g., no foreign transaction fee for the first 90 days)
- Seasonal fees: Fees can be activated/deactivated for specific date ranges DEFINING FEES BY TIME PERIOD:
- Each fee type supports start date and end date configuration
- Recurring fees support different amounts for different periods (e.g., $0 for months 1-12, then $95/year after)
- All time-based fee rules are set per product and can differ by FI CARD-LEVEL FEE CHANGES:
- Individual accounts can have fees waived, reduced, or modified with a reason code
- Fee override logged with audit trail
questions.paragraph: Yes. Fees can be configured with time-based rules and waived for defined periods. First-year annual fee waivers, introductory periods with reduced fees, and promotional waivers tied to campaigns are all configurable. Each fee type supports start date and end date configuration. Recurring fees can carry different amounts for different periods — for example, $0 for the first 12 months, then $95 annually. Time-based fee rules are set per product and can differ by agent bank FI. At the individual account level, fees can be waived, reduced, or modified with a reason code and audit trail. BSB can define seasonal fee rules that activate or deactivate for specific date ranges.

questions.requirement: Describe how you support statement file production, printing/delivery, and retention for all statement types.
questions.bullet:
Brim handles statement production, printing, delivery, and retention across all statement types. STATEMENT PRODUCTION:
- Monthly statements generated automatically per billing cycle
- Statement types: Regular monthly, annual summary, tax-related (e.g., 1099-INT), ad-hoc/on-demand
- Data: Transaction history, balance summary, minimum payment, due date, rewards summary, APR disclosure, fee summary PRINTING AND DELIVERY:
- Paper statements: Produced and mailed through Brim's print fulfillment partner
- Electronic delivery (eDelivery): Statements available in cardholder portal and mobile app
- Email notification: Alert sent when electronic statement is ready
- BSB can set eDelivery as default with paper opt-in, or paper as default with eDelivery opt-in RETENTION:
- Statement history retained and accessible online for a configurable period (e.g., 7 years)
- Archived statements retrievable on request beyond the online retention window
- Statements stored with encryption at rest (AES-256)
questions.paragraph: Brim handles statement production, printing, delivery, and retention. Monthly statements are generated automatically per billing cycle. Statement types include regular monthly, annual summary, tax-related (e.g., 1099-INT), and ad-hoc on-demand statements. Each statement includes transaction history, balance summary, minimum payment, due date, rewards summary, APR disclosure, and fee summary. Paper statements are produced and mailed through Brim's print fulfillment partner. Electronic statements are available in the cardholder portal and mobile app with email notification when ready. BSB configures the default delivery method — eDelivery with paper opt-in, or paper with eDelivery opt-in. Statement history is retained and accessible online for a configurable period, typically 7 years. Archived statements beyond the online window are retrievable on request. All stored statements are encrypted at rest with AES-256.

questions.requirement:
Describe your ability to customize statements to include:
 - Color statements 
 - Display customer FICO/credit score information on the statement 
 - Include marketing inserts
 - Add BSB/Client FI branding to the statement
questions.bullet:
Statements are customizable in content, design, and branding. CUSTOMIZATION OPTIONS:
- Color statements: Full-color supported for both paper and electronic
- FICO/credit score: Customer's credit score can be displayed on the statement per CARD Act section 1100F
- Marketing inserts: Paper statements support printed inserts. Electronic statements support embedded offers and banner messages
- BSB/Client FI branding: Logo, color scheme, contact information, and branding applied per agent bank FI
- Custom messaging: Regulatory disclosures, promotional messages, and personalized messages configurable per statement cycle
- Separate print and fulfillment management: BSB can direct print fulfillment to its preferred vendor or use Brim's partner
- Targeted communications: Statements can include segment-specific messages (e.g., delinquent account messaging, reward milestone notifications)
questions.paragraph: Statements are fully customizable. Color printing is supported for both paper and electronic formats. Customer FICO or credit score can be displayed on the statement in compliance with CARD Act section 1100F disclosure requirements. Paper statements support marketing inserts — printed materials included with the mailing. Electronic statements support embedded offers, banner messages, and personalized calls to action. BSB and agent bank FI branding — logo, color scheme, contact information — is applied per FI so each agent bank's cardholders receive statements branded to their institution. Custom messaging is configurable per statement cycle. Regulatory disclosures, promotional messages, delinquent account notices, and reward milestone notifications can be targeted by customer segment. BSB can direct print fulfillment to its preferred vendor or use Brim's partner.

questions.requirement: Describe how you accept customization of the statement layout, data, messaging, and message order. Describe your ability to personalize a statement for a client FI or company. Is it possible to differentiate statements by card type, business/consumer, BIN, BIN ranges, etc.? Please describe how.
questions.bullet:
Statement layout, data, messaging, and message order are all customizable. Personalization per FI and per company is supported. LAYOUT CUSTOMIZATION:
- Section order: BSB defines the sequence of statement sections (e.g., account summary first, then transactions, then rewards, then disclosures)
- Data fields: BSB selects which fields appear and where
- Message placement: Regulatory, promotional, and custom messages positioned per BSB specifications
- Font, color, and spacing are configurable within the statement template PER-FI PERSONALIZATION:
- Each agent bank FI can have its own statement template with unique branding, messaging, and layout
- Company-level customization: Business accounts can include company-specific data (authorized user spend breakdowns, department codes, cost center allocation) CONSUMER VS. BUSINESS DIFFERENCES:
- Consumer statements show individual rewards, payment history, and FICO score
- Business statements show company-level summary, authorized user detail, and expense categorization
- Both follow the same template engine — differences are configuration, not separate systems
questions.paragraph: Statement layout is configurable through the Brim admin portal. BSB defines the section order — account summary, transaction detail, rewards summary, disclosures — and chooses which data fields appear in each section. Message placement (regulatory, promotional, custom) is positioned per BSB's specifications. Font, color, and spacing are configurable within the template. Each agent bank FI can have its own statement template. Branding, messaging, layout, and content differ by FI. Business accounts can include company-specific data: authorized user spend breakdowns, department codes, and cost center allocation. Consumer statements show individual rewards, payment history, and credit score. Both consumer and business statements run through the same template engine. The differences are in the configuration, not in separate systems. BSB can preview and approve statement templates before they go into production.

- Balance buckets (purchases, cash advances, balance transfers, promotional) show their respective APRs
- If a cardholder holds two products (e.g., rewards card + business card), each product's statement shows that product's APRs
- Combined statements: If BSB offers a combined statement view, each product section shows its own APR schedule

questions.paragraph: Yes. When a cardholder holds multiple products, each product's statement displays its own APR schedule. The statement shows separate APRs for each balance bucket — purchases, cash advances, balance transfers, and promotional balances — specific to that product. If BSB offers a combined statement view spanning multiple products, each product section carries its own APR disclosure. CARD Act and Truth in Lending Act requirements are applied per product to ensure compliant disclosure.

questions.requirement:
Describe your ability to post/block:
 - Spend transactions
 - Cash withdrawal transactions
 - Details of foreign transactions including exchange rates and fees
 - Refund transactions received from consumers
 - Provisional credits for disputed transactions
 - Financial wellness information (for e.g., FICO information)
 - Installment/BNPL transactions, fees, APR, number of payments, etc.
 - Turn off ability for business card to buy bitcoin, etc.
questions.bullet:
The platform supports posting and blocking across all standard transaction types. POSTING CAPABILITIES:
- Spend transactions: Purchases post with merchant name, MCC, amount, date, and authorization reference
- Cash withdrawals: Cash advance transactions post with location, amount, and fee
- Foreign transactions: Post with original currency amount, exchange rate, converted amount, and foreign transaction fee
- Refund transactions: Merchant-initiated refunds post as credits
- Adjustment transactions: Manual or system adjustments for corrections, fee reversals, or promotional credits
- Dispute credits: Provisional and final credits posted during dispute lifecycle BLOCKING CAPABILITIES:
- Transaction type blocks: Block cash advances, balance transfers, or specific MCCs per card, product, or FI
- Geographic blocks: Block transactions from specific countries or regions
- Amount blocks: Block transactions above or below configurable thresholds
- All blocks configurable per card, account, product, or program-wide
questions.paragraph: The platform posts all standard transaction types: purchases (with merchant name, MCC, amount, date, and authorization reference), cash withdrawals (with location, amount, and fee), foreign transactions (with original currency, exchange rate, converted amount, and foreign transaction fee), refund transactions from merchants, manual adjustments, and dispute credits (provisional and final). Blocking is configurable by transaction type, geography, and amount. BSB can block cash advances, balance transfers, or specific MCCs at the card, product, or FI level. Geographic blocks restrict transactions from specific countries or regions. Amount blocks prevent transactions above or below configurable thresholds. Blocks apply per card, per account, per product, or program-wide depending on how BSB configures them.

questions.requirement: Describe your process for applying payments to balances. Include all payment methods currently processed. Can you support both immediate or postponed, and partial/interim payments to the account?
questions.bullet:
Payments apply to balances following CARD Act allocation rules. Multiple payment methods are supported. PAYMENT APPLICATION ORDER:
- Minimum payment: Applied to the lowest-APR balance first (per CARD Act)
- Excess over minimum: Applied to the highest-APR balance first (per CARD Act)
- BSB can confirm the allocation hierarchy during implementation PAYMENT METHODS:
- ACH: Direct debit from cardholder's bank account
- Online portal: Cardholder initiates payment through self-service portal
- Mobile app: Payment via mobile application
- Phone: IVR self-service or agent-assisted
- In-branch: Teller-initiated payment
- Wire transfer: For large or commercial payments
- Check: Processed upon receipt and posting IMMEDIATE AND POSTPONED PAYMENTS:
- Immediate: Payment posts on receipt (subject to hold policy)
- Scheduled/postponed: Cardholder or agent can schedule a future-dated payment
- Partial payments: Accepted and applied per allocation rules. Remaining balance carries forward
- Interim payments: Additional payments beyond the minimum accepted at any time
questions.paragraph: Payments are applied to balances following CARD Act allocation rules. The minimum payment amount applies to the lowest-APR balance first. Any amount above the minimum applies to the highest-APR balance first. BSB confirms the exact allocation hierarchy during implementation. Accepted payment methods include ACH, online portal, mobile app, phone (IVR and agent-assisted), in-branch, wire transfer, and check. The system supports both immediate and scheduled payments. Immediate payments post on receipt subject to the hold policy. Scheduled payments can be set for a future date. Partial payments are accepted and applied per the allocation rules — the remaining balance carries forward. Cardholders can make additional interim payments beyond the minimum at any time.

questions.requirement: Describe how you can calculate minimum payment. How is overlimit calculated (e.g., if balance is greater than assigned credit limit)? Do you support parameters around allowed overlimit?
questions.bullet:
Minimum payment calculation is configurable. Over-limit handling supports configurable parameters. MINIMUM PAYMENT:
- Calculation method: Configurable as a percentage of the statement balance, a flat dollar amount, or the greater of the two
- Components: Interest charges, fees, past-due amount, and over-limit amount can be included in or excluded from the minimum
- Floor: Configurable minimum dollar amount (e.g., $25 or the full balance if under $25)
- BSB defines the formula per product OVER-LIMIT:
- Over-limit occurs when the balance exceeds the assigned credit limit
- Over-limit is calculated as: current balance minus credit limit
- BSB configures whether over-limit transactions are allowed or declined at authorization
- If allowed, over-limit fee can be charged (requires cardholder opt-in per CARD Act for consumer)
- Over-limit tolerance: Configurable buffer above the credit limit (e.g., allow up to 10% over-limit)
- Over-limit amount is included in the next minimum payment calculation
questions.paragraph: Minimum payment calculation is configurable per product. BSB defines the formula: a percentage of the statement balance, a flat dollar amount, or the greater of the two. Interest charges, fees, past-due amounts, and over-limit amounts can be included in or excluded from the calculation. A minimum dollar floor applies — for example, $25 or the full balance if under $25. Over-limit is calculated as the current balance minus the assigned credit limit. BSB configures whether over-limit transactions are approved or declined at authorization. If approved, an over-limit fee can be charged, though consumer accounts require cardholder opt-in per CARD Act. A configurable over-limit tolerance can be set — for example, allow transactions up to 10% above the credit limit. The over-limit amount rolls into the next minimum payment calculation.

questions.requirement: Describe how you support recurring ACH/auto pay set up? How can cardholders add/edit/cancel payments and ensure it takes effect on current cycle rather than next cycle? Describe how you support the communication to customers regarding setup for preauthorized/auto pay arrangements. Describe how you support discounts or fee waivers for enrolling or maintaining autopay.
questions.bullet:
Recurring ACH / auto-pay is supported with cardholder self-service for setup, edits, and cancellation. AUTO-PAY SETUP:
- Cardholders set up auto-pay through the online portal, mobile app, or by phone
- Payment options: Minimum payment, statement balance, fixed amount, or full balance
- Source: Cardholder's linked bank account via ACH
- Timing: Payment debited on configurable schedule (e.g., due date, X days before due date, specific calendar date) EDITS AND CANCELLATION:
- Cardholders can add, edit, or cancel auto-pay through the same channels (portal, mobile, phone)
- Changes made before the current cycle's processing cutoff take effect on the current cycle
- Changes after cutoff apply to the next cycle
- Confirmation notification sent for every auto-pay setup, edit, or cancellation ADDITIONAL CAPABILITIES:
- Convenience fees: Configurable for phone payments or expedited payments
- Pay-by-text: If configured, cardholder can initiate payment via SMS command
- Payment confirmation: Real-time confirmation via portal, email, SMS, or push notification This capability runs in production across all Brim programs — processing transactions for Affinity CU, Manulife, UNI, Zolve, and Continental Currency Exchange with 99.97% uptime over the past 12 months.
questions.paragraph: Recurring ACH and auto-pay are fully supported. Cardholders set up auto-pay through the online portal, mobile app, or by phone. Payment options include minimum payment, statement balance, fixed amount, or full balance, debited from the cardholder's linked bank account on a configurable schedule. Cardholders can edit or cancel auto-pay through the same channels. Changes made before the current cycle's processing cutoff take effect on the current cycle — not the next cycle. Changes after the cutoff apply to the next cycle. Confirmation notifications are sent for every setup, edit, and cancellation. Convenience fees are configurable for phone payments or expedited payments. Pay-by-text can be enabled so cardholders initiate a payment via SMS command. Payment confirmation is delivered in real time via the portal, email, SMS, or push notification.

questions.requirement: Describe how you enable BSB to manually enter credit card payments and override any calculated payments as needed? If manual payment is entered, how can the manual payment override the UI with reason codes, validation (amount/date), and supervisory approval rules?
questions.bullet:
BSB staff can manually enter payments and override calculated payment amounts through the admin portal. MANUAL PAYMENT ENTRY:
- Call center agents and authorized staff enter payments directly in the admin portal
- Payment types: One-time payment, payment reversal, payment adjustment
- Override: The manual payment amount can override the calculated minimum payment or scheduled auto-pay amount
- Reason code required: Every manual payment or override requires a reason code from a configurable list
- Notes: Free-text field for additional context UI BEHAVIOR:
- When a manual payment is entered, the system updates the UI to reflect the override with the reason code displayed
- The calculated payment amount remains visible alongside the manual override for audit purposes
- OTB updates immediately upon manual payment posting CONTROLS:
- Manual payment authority is role-based — only authorized agents can enter or override payments
- All manual entries logged with agent ID, timestamp, amount, reason code, and notes
- Override thresholds: BSB can set limits on manual payment amounts that require supervisor approval
questions.paragraph: BSB staff can manually enter credit card payments and override calculated payment amounts through the admin portal. Authorized agents enter payments directly — one-time payments, payment reversals, or payment adjustments. When a manual payment overrides the calculated minimum or a scheduled auto-pay amount, the system requires a reason code from a configurable list and supports a free-text notes field. The UI reflects the override with the reason code displayed alongside the original calculated amount for audit purposes. OTB updates immediately when the manual payment posts. Manual payment authority is role-based. Only authorized agents can enter or override payments. All manual entries are logged with agent ID, timestamp, amount, reason code, and notes. BSB can configure override thresholds that require supervisor approval above a certain dollar amount.

questions.requirement: Describe your ability to support customizable outreach and channels (push notifications, SMS, phone, and email). Describe how you support customizable outreach per rule/trigger (e.g., medium-risk 3DS challenge, new device login, digital wallet provisioning).
questions.bullet: Brim supports configurable outreach across push notifications, SMS, email, and in-app channels, with 150+ notification types that can be mapped to specific triggers. Each rule or event — such as a 3DS challenge, new device login, or digital wallet provisioning — can be assigned its own notification channel and message template, configurable by BSB without engineering involvement.
questions.paragraph: Brim's notification engine supports 150+ distinct notification types that can be delivered via SMS, email, push notification, or in-app message. Each notification is tied to a specific trigger or rule, and BSB can configure which channel is used for each event type. For example, a medium-risk 3DS challenge can trigger an SMS OTP, a new device login can generate an email alert plus push notification, and a digital wallet provisioning event can send an in-app confirmation. Notification templates are fully customizable by BSB through the partner administration portal — no code changes needed. Real-time fraud SMS alerts are also built in: when a suspicious transaction is detected, the cardholder receives an SMS within seconds and can confirm or deny the charge directly. Channel preferences can also be set at the cardholder level, allowing customers to choose how they want to be contacted for non-mandatory communications.

BSB administrators can also notate contact preferences at the account level through the partner portal.

Brim will confirm the specific delivery milestone during implementation planning.

The call center connects directly to Brim's processing system. Agents see real-time account data, transaction history, and card status. Over 300 servicing functions are available from the agent portal — card activation, payment processing, dispute initiation, account changes — without switching systems. The contact center is US-based and provides phone, email, chat, and secure messaging, with 24/7/365 support for lost/stolen cards and fraud.

questions.paragraph: Brim's platform supports single sign-on federation through SAML 2.0, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, and JWT tokens. This means a cardholder authenticated in Brim's white-labeled portal can be passed into Banno or other BSB applications without a separate login. The SSO configuration is set up during implementation and can be tailored to BSB's identity provider and session management requirements. BSB controls which applications participate in the SSO chain and what user attributes are passed in the token. The integration works in both directions — a user authenticated in Banno can also be federated into the Brim cardholder portal through the same protocols.

questions.paragraph: Brim's transaction search is available across the cardholder web portal, mobile app, and agent portal for customer service representatives. Cardholders and agents can search transactions by merchant name, category, date range, amount, or custom tags. Each transaction record shows how it was initiated — chip, contactless tap, e-commerce, mobile wallet, or card-not-present — along with the authorization status, merchant category code, and whether it posted or is still pending. Transactions appear in real time as they are authorized, so there is no delay between a purchase and when it shows up in the search results. Users can filter by foreign transactions, fees and interest, payments, refunds, or recurring charges. Transaction data can be exported in CSV, XLS, or PDF formats for record-keeping or reconciliation.

questions.requirement: Describe your consumer and small business servicing platform.
questions.bullet: Brim's servicing platform covers both consumer and small business credit card programs through a single technology stack. Consumer cardholders get self-service account management, while business cardholders get additional features like expense management, multi-card controls, and department-level reporting.
questions.paragraph: Brim operates a unified servicing platform for both consumer and small business credit card programs. On the consumer side, the platform provides self-service capabilities including card lock/unlock, PIN reset, payment scheduling, transaction alerts, digital wallet provisioning, balance transfers, autopay, and paperless statements — all through a white-labeled, BSB-branded portal and mobile app. On the small business side, the platform adds expense management, multi-card controls, department-level reporting, authorized user management, and single-use virtual card issuance. Both segments are supported by Brim's US-based contact center with phone, email, chat, and secure messaging. The agent portal gives customer service representatives access to over 300 servicing functions across both consumer and business accounts. Business administrators get their own portal with role-based access to manage cardholders, set spending limits, and view company-wide transaction reporting.

questions.requirement: Describe if you support/provide an online contact center tool (e.g., GLIA) to provide real-time assistance to customers.  Describe your ability to support omnichannel complaint management (online, app, phone, etc.)
questions.bullet: Yes. Brim supports integration with online contact center tools including Glia for real-time customer assistance. The Glia integration — currently in implementation with go-live targeted for Q3 2026 — will provide co-browsing, video chat, screen sharing, and AI-powered chatbot capabilities within the cardholder portal. In the interim, Brim's existing digital support includes live chat, secure messaging, and callback scheduling, all integrated with the CSR desktop for seamless customer interactions.
questions.paragraph:
BSB uses Glia for online contact center support, and Brim has committed to integrating with Glia as part of BSB's implementation, targeting operational readiness at go-live. The integration gives BSB's representatives real-time chat and co-browsing within the credit card portal. Brim's APIs feed account data, fraud alerts, and transaction context into the Glia session so agents have full context without asking for account numbers.

For complaint management, Brim supports intake across all channels — web portal, mobile app, phone, and secure messaging. All complaints are logged centrally in the agent portal with status, timestamps, and assigned owner. Agents see the full history regardless of which channel the complaint came through. Escalation workflows route by type and severity, and BSB administrators pull reports on volumes, resolution times, and outcomes.

The Glia integration is API-based, following the same pattern as Brim's existing integrations with Fiserv, Collabria, i2c, and TransUnion. Brim will confirm the delivery milestone during implementation planning.

Brim's omnichannel complaint management is live for AFFINITY CREDIT UNION and MANULIFE today. The Glia integration adds BSB's preferred digital engagement layer on top of Brim's existing contact center infrastructure.

Context-sensitive help is accessible from every screen in the portal, including searchable knowledge base articles and process documentation. BSB can also create custom help content and troubleshooting guides that appear alongside standard Brim documentation. questions.requirement:
Describe how you can support the following controls and how BSB employees and program admin can manage/change/reset controls if needed:
 - Daily, weekly, monthly limits and time of day (e.g., 9 to 5),
 - Cash/purchase, cash advance limit,
 - Checking amount limit and/or count of transactions,
 - Number of transactions,
 - Single purchase transaction limits, and
 - MCC - An unlimited amount of MCCs/MCGs
questions.bullet: Brim supports configurable spending controls including daily, weekly, and monthly limits, time-of-day restrictions, cash advance limits, transaction count limits, and merchant category restrictions. BSB employees and program administrators can manage and reset these controls through the partner portal. These features are live across Brim's programs serving cardholders for Affinity CU, Manulife (55,000 advisors), UNI Financial, and Zolve with 24/7 digital access and real-time notifications.
questions.paragraph: Brim's platform supports a full set of configurable spending controls at both the account and card level. BSB can set daily, weekly, and monthly spending limits, as well as time-of-day restrictions (e.g., transactions allowed only between 9 AM and 5 PM). Cash advance and purchase limits are configured separately, and transaction count limits can be applied per time period. Merchant category code (MCC) restrictions allow BSB to block or allow specific merchant categories per card. Geographic restrictions can limit transactions to domestic-only or block specific countries. BSB employees can view, change, and reset these controls through the partner portal based on their role permissions. Cardholders can also manage certain controls through self-service — including card lock/unlock, blocking foreign or online transactions, and setting spending limits on authorized user cards. All control changes take effect in real time and are logged in the audit trail.

questions.paragraph: Brim's platform supports bulk updates to cardholder data fields through batch file processing. BSB can submit a formatted file containing changes to any account data field — contact information, account settings, card controls, or relationship attributes — and Brim processes the updates on a scheduled or on-demand basis. This applies to consumer, small business, and corporate accounts. For relationship-level changes (e.g., updating a billing address that affects multiple cards under one account), the portal and API both support cascading updates. Individual maintenance requests can also be submitted through the partner portal UI or via API calls from BSB's own systems. All changes are validated against data integrity rules before processing, and results are reported back with success/failure status for each record. The batch process supports standard file formats and can be automated on a recurring schedule. Brim's customer platform serves cardholders across multiple live programs with 24/7 digital access, real-time notifications, and self-service account management.
