questions.requirement: Describe your ability to close, restore, and reinstate cardholder accounts. Describe your ability to provide reason codes for account closures. Describe your ability to automate the flow of information to and from Jack Henry to ensure automatic closure of non-joint account following SSA notification that a borrower is deceased
questions.bullet: Yes. Brim supports full account lifecycle management including close, restore, and reinstate actions with configurable reason codes. Account closure workflows include balance verification, reward forfeiture/preservation rules, and automated adverse action notices. Restoration is supported within BSB-defined time windows (e.g., 30 days post-closure). Reinstatement includes re-underwriting triggers configurable by BSB. All actions are logged with reason codes, timestamps, and operator IDs for audit compliance. Reason code lists are fully customizable by BSB.
questions.paragraph:
Brim supports closing, restoring, and reinstating cardholder accounts through the partner portal and API. When an account is closed, BSB selects from configurable reason codes — voluntary, involuntary (collections), fraud, deceased, or other BSB-defined categories. All codes are tracked and reportable for portfolio analysis.

Restoration of recently closed accounts and reinstatement of charged-off accounts are both supported with approval workflows that the system enforces. For SSA death notification handling, the Jack Henry integration is scoped for BSB's implementation — once built, the system receives the notification through Jack Henry, identifies the credit card account, and initiates closure with the correct reason code and correspondence. Until the integration is live, BSB operations processes SSA notifications manually through the portal.

Account closure and reinstatement workflows are live across Brim's production portfolio. AFFINITY CREDIT UNION and MANULIFE use reason-coded closures with full audit trails today. UNI's migration of 80,000 cards from Collabria required account status mapping — including closed and charged-off accounts — handled through this system.

questions.requirement: Describe your dynamic portfolio capabilities including enablement of Apple Wallet, Google Wallet, installment payment by purchase or product.
questions.bullet: Brim supports Apple Wallet and Google Wallet provisioning with one-click activation from the mobile app. The platform also supports installment payments (BNPL) that can be applied to individual purchases or offered as a product-level feature, with configurable terms from 3 to 48 cycles.
questions.paragraph: Brim's platform supports digital wallet provisioning for Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay, Garmin Pay, and Fitbit Pay. Cardholders can provision their card to a mobile wallet with a single tap from the mobile app, and when a lost or stolen card is replaced, the new virtual card can be added to the wallet automatically with no downtime. For installment payments, Brim offers an embedded BNPL feature that is merchant-agnostic — cardholders can split any purchase into installments directly from the portal or mobile app, either before or after the transaction. Installment plans are configurable from 3 to 48 payment cycles, and BSB controls the pricing and fee structure. The installment feature can be offered as a product-level capability or enabled selectively by customer segment. Both wallet provisioning and installment payment activity are visible in real time through the cardholder portal and BSB's partner portal.

questions.paragraph: PIN reset is fully managed by Brim with no action required from BSB. questions.requirement: Describe your ability to step-up authentication including a One Time Password (OTP) for sensitive actions. Describe your ability for this service to be customized.
questions.bullet: Brim supports step-up authentication with a 6-digit SMS OTP or a PIN set at card activation. Step-up is triggered for sensitive actions like viewing card details, changing personal info, and adding authorized users. BSB can customize which actions require step-up.
questions.paragraph: Brim's platform uses step-up authentication for sensitive cardholder actions. Two options are available: a 6-digit SMS one-time password (OTP) or a PIN that the cardholder sets during card activation. Cardholders can switch from SMS to PIN for added security, and once on PIN, switching back to SMS is blocked to prevent SIM swap attacks. Step-up authentication is triggered for actions such as viewing or changing personal information, viewing full card details, adding authorized users, downloading statements, and changing the PIN. BSB can customize which specific actions require step-up and configure the thresholds. After too many failed OTP or PIN attempts, the card is blocked and the cardholder is notified by email and SMS to contact support. Once authenticated, the cardholder can access sensitive pages for the duration of their session until logout or inactivity timeout.

questions.requirement: How do you enable BSB bank admin access to multiple instances to provide account servicing support to customers across client FI? Describe how you provide ease of access for BSB admin while ensuring data segregation and privacy between instances.
questions.bullet: BSB administrators can access multiple client FI instances from a single login to provide cross-FI account servicing. Data segregation between FIs is enforced at the database level with logical isolation and role-based access controls, so agents only see data for the FIs they are authorized to support.
questions.paragraph: Brim's multi-tenant architecture allows BSB administrators to service cardholders across multiple client FIs from a single partner portal login. When an agent searches for a customer, they see results only from the FIs their role is authorized to access. Data segregation between FIs is enforced through logical isolation at the database level — each FI's data is encrypted and segregated. Role-based access controls ensure that an agent authorized for one FI cannot view or modify accounts belonging to another FI unless explicitly permissioned. BSB can create role configurations that grant access to specific FI portfolios, making it straightforward to onboard new FIs without creating separate systems. The portal interface makes it clear which FI context the agent is operating in, reducing the risk of cross-FI errors.

questions.bullet: Brim's online card management tool provides self-service controls including card on/off, travel alerts, merchant category restrictions, geographic restrictions, transaction type controls, and spending limits. questions.paragraph: Brim's self-service card controls are available through the cardholder web portal and mobile app, and are also exposed through RESTful APIs for BSB to embed in its own digital channels. Available controls include: card lock/unlock (on/off), travel alerts, merchant category (MCC) allow/deny lists, geographic restrictions (domestic only, block specific countries), transaction type controls (block ATM, block online, block foreign), and per-card spending limits. Cardholders can also block cash advances, set up real-time purchase notifications, and manage authorized user card controls from the same interface. Changes take effect in real time — if a cardholder locks their card, the next attempted transaction is declined within seconds. BSB can configure which controls are available to cardholders by product type. questions.requirement: Describe your ability to provide self-service IVR for customers for actions such as balance, transactions, card controls.
questions.bullet: Yes. Brim provides self-service IVR capabilities for cardholders including: balance inquiry, recent transaction review, payment posting, card activation, PIN reset, card lock/unlock, and dispute initiation. The IVR supports natural language processing for intuitive navigation and integrates with Brim's platform for real-time account data. BSB configures the IVR menu structure, greeting messages, and routing rules. The system handles both touchtone and voice input with seamless transfer to live agents when needed.
questions.paragraph:
Brim's IVR system provides self-service for common cardholder needs, available 24/7/365. Cardholders check their balance (current balance, available credit, minimum payment, due date), review recent transactions, activate new or replacement cards, and reset their PIN — all without reaching a live agent.

The IVR pulls real-time data through Brim's APIs, so information is current. For security, sensitive actions require OTP authentication. The IVR handles fraud resolution: it plays back recent transactions and the cardholder confirms or denies each. If all are confirmed, the block lifts automatically. If any are denied, the call transfers to a fraud specialist. High-risk accounts flagged with certain fraud statuses bypass self-service and route directly to a fraud agent.

IVR uptime targets 98.5% with a 97% contractual minimum. The system is live for Brim's production portfolio — AFFINITY CREDIT UNION and MANULIFE cardholders use it for balance checks and card activation today.

questions.requirement:
Describe your collections and recovery management tools, including:

-Collections platform & vendors: provided software, SSO support, and browser compatibility
-Lifecycle management: ability to configure rules, workflows, and strategies across the full collections/recovery lifecycle
-Account visibility: how accounts in collections are displayed or flagged for users to assist  with managing the customer relationship through the entire collections/recovery lifecycle (e.g., setting rules and other options)?
questions.bullet:
- Brim runs a 6-bucket automated collections engine (PD01 through PD06, covering 1 to 180+ days past due) built directly into the card platform — not a bolt-on module.
- The collections workflow is browser-based, SSO-enabled, and accessible through the same Issuer Portal BSB uses for all other card management functions. No separate software install required.
- Lifecycle rules are configurable through the Brim admin portal by BSB: outreach type (call, email, SMS, letter, IVR), timing, card/account blocking thresholds, payment plan offers, charge-off triggers, and recovery handoff — all settable by product, consumer vs. business, and risk segment.
- Account visibility spans the full collections lifecycle in a single view: current delinquency bucket, contact history, payment arrangements, blocking status, and prior workout outcomes.
- Dynamic queuing prioritizes accounts by risk score, balance, days past due, and configurable BSB-defined criteria. A dedicated third-party collections partner handles late-stage recovery (post-PD03).
- Manulife, UNI (80K cards), and Affinity CU all run on this collections infrastructure today.
questions.paragraph: Brim's collections engine is built into the card platform — a 6-bucket system (PD01-PD06, 1-180+ days) with configurable rules for outreach, blocking, payment plans, charge-off, and recovery. BSB accesses it through the same browser-based, SSO-enabled Issuer Portal used for all card management. Account visibility spans the full lifecycle in a single view: delinquency bucket, contact history, payment arrangements, blocking status, and prior outcomes. Dynamic queuing prioritizes by risk score, balance, and days past due. Manulife, UNI, and Affinity CU run this infrastructure in production.

questions.requirement:
Please describe your collection module's capabilities to do/have the following:

- Integrate with core
- Be within a single payment class platform
-Make payment arrangements (Customize special pay), 
- Calculators built in (APR, min payment, etc.)
- Proactive management (e.g., missed first payment -> tool automatically checks on it/flags)
- Insight into all payments (e.g., seeing if someone made an online payment)
- Dynamic queuing (e.g., high risk addressed first, customers with existing bank relationship addressed separately) 
- The capability for admins to set specific rules and strategies in the platform
- Communication - ability to text, automated email
questions.bullet: Yes. Brim's collections module provides end-to-end capabilities including: core system integration (Jack Henry API), single-platform account management, configurable workflow queues by delinquency bucket, automated dialer integration, payment arrangement tools (promise-to-pay, hardship programs), skip tracing data feeds, letter/email/SMS communication sequences, agent productivity dashboards, and full regulatory compliance (FDCPA, TCPA, state-specific requirements). The module supports both internal collection teams and third-party agency placement with automated account assignment and performance tracking.
questions.paragraph:
The collections module is native to Brim's platform — no separate integration. Account data, payment history, and cardholder profile are available in real time. Collections, servicing, payments, and account management all run on one platform with no reconciliation gaps. Jack Henry SilverLake integration, scoped for BSB, syncs data bidirectionally.

Built-in calculators handle APR, minimum payment, payoff projections, and fee computations for both cardholder-facing displays and agent screens. Payment arrangements — custom pay plans, hardship programs, settlement offers — are configurable by BSB with system-enforced guardrails on maximum term and minimum payment. Cardholders view and manage arrangements through self-service.

Proactive management starts before delinquency: PD01 sends email reminders at -5, +1, and +25 days. First-payment-default calls go to new accounts. The system focuses proactive controls on recent vintage (12 months or less), credit limits at or above $3K, utilization at or above 40%, and payment factors at or below 30%. Accounts auto-block at 30 days past due. Delinquent accounts exceeding BSB's thresholds route to third-party agencies via data feeds.

Reporting covers delinquency buckets (30/60/90/120/150/180+ days) with drill-down, roll rates, cure rates, and loss forecasting — segmentable by product, agent bank, and vintage.

This module is live for AFFINITY CREDIT UNION and MANULIFE. Proactive delinquency triggers run daily across the production portfolio.

questions.requirement:
Describe the strategies your platform supports for managing delinquent accounts (e.g., outreach, card blocking, account blocking, payment plans, account closure, write-off). How can these strategies be configured by BSB (e.g., by product, consumer v. business, etc.)?

-Include how the platform supports the ability to block business cards specific to the business billing cycle or dpd depending on the business's billing liability (e.g., block based on 1 card past due, block a card but not the business account, or block the business account)?
questions.bullet:
- Strategies are configurable by product (consumer, business, secured, co-brand), customer segment (consumer vs. business), risk tier, and agent bank client FI.
- PD01 (1-30 days): automated email reminders at -5, +1, +25 days from due date. First-payment-default call for new accounts. Auto-block at 30% utilization with risk signals.
- PD02 (31-60 days): collection letters mailed at 40 and 50 days. Daily outbound calls begin at day 35. Account flagged for enhanced monitoring.
- PD03 (61-90 days): soft block applied at day 61 — cardholder cannot make new purchases but existing payment plans continue. Escalation to senior collector.
- PD04-PD05 (91-150 days): continued outreach with escalating urgency. Settlement offers and hardship programs available at BSB's discretion.
- PD06 (151-180 days): charge-off letter sent at day 165. Account revoked at day 181. Placement with recovery agency follows automatically.
- Bust-out detection: accounts showing rapid balance build-up require 50% balance payment before unblocking; credit limit is reduced and account may be closed.
questions.paragraph: Brim supports configurable delinquency strategies by product, consumer vs. business, risk tier, and client FI. The 6-bucket system runs from PD01 (email reminders at -5/+1/+25 days, first-payment-default calls) through PD06 (charge-off letter at day 165, account revocation at day 181). Soft blocks apply at day 61, daily outbound calls start at day 35, and collection letters go out at days 40 and 50. Bust-out detection requires 50% balance payment before unblocking and triggers limit reduction. BSB controls the timing, thresholds, and escalation paths for each bucket.

Describe your ability to support delinquency after Bangor-defined number of late payments, including delinquency history. Please include how days past due (DPD) is calculated.
questions.bullet:
- The system identifies any account not paid in full by the statement due date as delinquent and immediately begins the PD01 workflow. This applies to both consumer and business accounts.
- BSB can define the number of late payments that trigger escalation to each delinquency bucket — the 6-bucket structure (PD01-PD06) is the default, but bucket entry criteria are configurable.
- Full delinquency history is retained at the account level: every bucket transition, every contact attempt, every payment received, every blocking action, and every workout arrangement.
- Data flows to credit bureau reporting in real time — delinquency status updates are reflected in the next Metro 2 file cycle. Payment curing (account brought current) updates the delinquency status immediately.
- BSB's collections team and agent bank client FIs can view delinquency history in the Issuer Portal, filtered by product, customer segment, client FI, and date range.
questions.paragraph: Any account not paid by the due date is flagged as delinquent and enters the PD01 workflow immediately, for both consumer and business. BSB defines the criteria that trigger each bucket transition. Full delinquency history is retained at the account level — bucket transitions, contact attempts, payments, blocking actions, and workouts. Status changes flow to Metro 2 reporting in the next cycle, and payment curing updates status immediately. Delinquency history is viewable in the Issuer Portal by product, segment, client FI, and date range.

questions.requirement: Describe your ability to adjust the collections modules based how successful  communication  channels are on a customer and entity basis. This includes automated letters, email, IVR, dialers, etc.
questions.bullet:
- Brim's collections engine tracks contact success rates by channel (email, letter, IVR, outbound call, SMS) at the customer and entity level.
- Channel effectiveness data — open rates, response rates, promise-to-pay conversion, and actual payment received — feeds back into the strategy engine so BSB can adjust outreach sequencing.
- If a cardholder consistently responds to email but ignores calls, the system can prioritize email-first outreach for that account. BSB defines the rules; the platform executes them.
- At the entity level (for business accounts or agent bank client FI cohorts), BSB can set different channel mixes based on aggregate response patterns.
- Automated letters are generated and mailed on a configurable schedule. Email and IVR templates are BSB-branded and editable through the Issuer Portal. Dialer integration supports outbound call campaigns with priority queuing.
questions.paragraph: Brim tracks contact success rates by channel — email open rates, call answer rates, IVR completion, letter response, and promise-to-pay conversion — at both the customer and entity level. This data feeds the strategy engine, so BSB can adjust outreach sequencing based on what actually works for each account. Automated letters, BSB-branded email/IVR templates, and dialer integration with priority queuing are all configurable through the Issuer Portal.

questions.requirement: Describe your ability to offer and support payment plans in the collections process. Are there out of the box payment plans and/or customizable plans? Describe how settlements are managed and supported.
questions.bullet:
- Brim provides out-of-box payment plan templates: fixed monthly payment over 6/12/18/24 months, interest-rate-reduced hardship plans, and minimum-payment-reset arrangements.
- BSB can also create custom plan structures — defining the payment amount, frequency, duration, interest rate during the plan, and what happens if the cardholder misses a plan payment (re-age, re-default, or escalate).
- Settlements (lump-sum payoff at a reduced balance) are supported with configurable approval thresholds. BSB defines what percentage of balance can be accepted as settlement at each delinquency stage.
- Settlement approval can be tiered by authority level — for example, a collector can approve settlements above 80% of balance, a supervisor above 60%, and anything below 60% requires BSB management approval.
- All plan and settlement terms are documented in the system with audit trails, and confirmation letters are automatically generated and sent to the cardholder.
questions.paragraph: Brim offers out-of-box payment plans (6/12/18/24-month fixed payment, hardship rate reductions, minimum payment resets) and lets BSB create custom structures with defined amounts, frequencies, durations, and miss-payment consequences. Settlements are supported with configurable approval thresholds by delinquency stage and authority level — for example, collectors approve above 80%, supervisors above 60%, management below that. All terms are audit-logged and confirmation letters are generated automatically.

questions.requirement: How do you handle/permit re-aging (automated and manual) of an account?
questions.bullet:
- Brim supports both automated and manual re-aging. Automated re-aging triggers when a delinquent account makes a qualifying payment (amount and timing defined by BSB) — the system resets the delinquency counter and returns the account to current status.
- Manual re-aging is available to authorized BSB users through the Issuer Portal. The system enforces configurable guardrails: maximum number of re-ages per account per 12-month period, minimum payment required before re-aging, and minimum days between re-age events.
- Re-aging rules can differ by product, consumer vs. business, and delinquency bucket. BSB controls all thresholds.
- Every re-age event — automated or manual — is logged with the account number, date, authorizing user (for manual), reason code, and the delinquency status before and after.
- Re-aged accounts are flagged in reporting so BSB can track re-age frequency and monitor for re-age abuse or policy exceptions.
questions.paragraph: Brim supports automated re-aging (triggered when a qualifying payment is received, per BSB-defined thresholds) and manual re-aging by authorized users through the Issuer Portal. Guardrails are system-enforced: maximum re-ages per 12-month period, minimum payment required, and minimum days between events. Rules differ by product and delinquency bucket. Every re-age is logged with account, date, authorizing user, reason code, and status change. Re-aged accounts are flagged in reporting for frequency monitoring.

questions.requirement:
Does the system prioritize and manage delinquent accounts? Please describe the following, if applicable:
 - Does the system follow criteria considering delinquency status management?
 - Does the system support any other method considering delinquency status management? Please describe. 
 - What are the supported hierarchy/segmentation levels for setting up the condition parameters? (employee, high risk, repeat delinquency, etc.) Describe your ability to prioritize segments to enforce separate strategies. 
 - Is it an automated process?
 - Is manual set up allowed/required?
questions.bullet:
- Yes, the system prioritizes delinquent accounts using a multi-factor scoring model: days past due, outstanding balance, risk score, account vintage, utilization rate, payment history pattern, and BSB-defined priority flags.
- The system follows a 6-bucket delinquency status framework (PD01-PD06) with escalating actions at each stage. Bucket assignment drives the collection strategy: outreach frequency, channel mix, blocking actions, and workout eligibility.
- Beyond bucket status, the system supports behavioral triggers: first-payment default, sudden utilization spike (bust-out detection), skipped-payment pattern, and minimum-payment-only pattern.
- BSB can configure priority overrides — for example, flagging all business accounts with balances over $10K for immediate supervisor review regardless of bucket status.
- Proactive risk controls target accounts with recent vintage (12 months or less), credit limits at or above $3K, utilization at or above 40%, and payment factor at or below 30% — catching problems before they reach PD02.
- Dynamic queue rebalancing ensures agents work the highest-priority accounts first, recalculated daily.
questions.paragraph: The system prioritizes delinquent accounts using days past due, balance, risk score, vintage, utilization, and payment pattern. The 6-bucket framework (PD01-PD06) drives escalating actions at each stage. Beyond bucket status, behavioral triggers catch first-payment defaults, bust-out patterns, and utilization spikes. Proactive controls target recent vintage accounts (12 months or less on book) with utilization above 40% and payment factor below 30%. BSB can configure priority overrides, and queues rebalance daily so agents always work the highest-priority accounts first.

questions.requirement: Describe your ability to automatically charge off balances with no manual intervention. What customization is available for BSB (e.g., set/edit based on timing). Describe the process to complete charge off post-collection. Is the post-collection process manual?
questions.bullet:
- Brim automates charge-off with no manual intervention required. At PD06, a charge-off letter is sent at day 165, and the account is revoked at day 181 — per the standard 180-day contractual charge-off timeline.
- BSB can configure the charge-off timing: the 165-day letter and 181-day revocation are defaults, but both dates are adjustable by product, consumer vs. business, and client FI.
- The charge-off process automatically: closes the account, updates the account status, writes off the balance to the GL, generates the Metro 2 charge-off reporting code, and places the account with the recovery agency.
- Post-collection charge-off (where partial recovery is received after charge-off) updates the GL and Metro 2 records automatically. The recovery amount is credited, the remaining balance is adjusted, and the bureau file reflects the updated status.
- BSB receives automated charge-off summary reports (daily and monthly) with full detail on each charged-off account.
questions.paragraph: Charge-off runs automatically at PD06: a charge-off letter goes out at day 165 and the account is revoked at day 181, with no manual intervention. BSB can adjust both dates by product and segment. The system automatically closes the account, writes off the balance to the GL, generates the Metro 2 charge-off code, and places the account with the recovery agency. Post-charge-off recoveries update the GL and Metro 2 records automatically. BSB receives daily and monthly charge-off summary reports.

questions.requirement: Describe the collections integrations with your rewards solution and describe how rewards can be excluded from redemption in the collections process.
questions.bullet:
- Brim's rewards engine is integrated with the collections workflow. When an account enters a BSB-defined delinquency bucket (configurable — typically PD02 or PD03), rewards redemption is automatically suspended.
- Suspended rewards points/cashback remain on the account but cannot be redeemed until the account is brought current and exits the delinquency status.
- BSB defines the rules: which delinquency bucket triggers suspension, whether earned-but-unredeemed rewards are forfeited at charge-off or retained, and whether rewards accrual continues during delinquency.
- If the account is charged off, BSB can configure whether accumulated rewards are forfeited or applied as a credit against the outstanding balance before write-off.
- All rewards suspension and forfeiture events are logged and visible in the account-level collections history.
questions.paragraph: Brim's rewards engine integrates with collections. When an account hits a BSB-defined delinquency bucket (typically PD02 or PD03), rewards redemption is automatically suspended — points remain on the account but cannot be redeemed until the account is current. BSB controls the rules: suspension trigger bucket, whether accrual continues during delinquency, and whether accumulated rewards are forfeited or credited against the balance at charge-off. All suspension and forfeiture events are logged in the account-level collections history.

questions.requirement: Describe the solutions ability to provide separate bank user account levels and access roles for collections. For example, specific roles or employees are allowed to view/edit/manage a specific segment of accounts.
questions.bullet:
- Brim's Issuer Portal supports granular role-based access control (RBAC) for collections. BSB defines roles — collector, senior collector, collections supervisor, collections manager, compliance reviewer — each with specific view, edit, and action permissions.
- Account segment access can be restricted by role: for example, a junior collector sees only PD01-PD02 accounts, while a senior collector handles PD03-PD05. Supervisors see all accounts but only they can approve settlements below a defined threshold.
- In BSB's agent bank model, access is also partitioned by client FI. A collections agent assigned to one client FI cannot view or act on another FI's accounts unless explicitly granted cross-FI access.
- User permissions cover specific actions: initiate payment plan, approve settlement, apply re-age, override block, escalate to supervisor, place with agency. Each action can be enabled or disabled per role.
- All access and actions are audit-logged with user ID, timestamp, and action detail.
questions.paragraph: Brim's Issuer Portal supports granular RBAC for collections. BSB defines roles (collector, supervisor, manager, compliance reviewer) with specific view/edit/action permissions and account segment restrictions — a junior collector might see only PD01-PD02, while supervisors approve settlements. In the agent bank model, access is partitioned by client FI so collectors cannot view other institutions' accounts without explicit cross-FI access. Specific actions (initiate plan, approve settlement, apply re-age, place with agency) are individually permission-controlled, and all activity is audit-logged.

questions.paragraph: Brim's billing engine produces itemized invoices at the partner FI level with full transparency: Fixed charges: Platform fees, BIN maintenance, card program management, compliance and regulatory services — each line item is identified separately with the contracted rate. Variable charges: Transaction processing (per-transaction fee x volume), card production (per-card fee x cards issued), statement generation (per-statement fee x statements produced), dispute handling (per-dispute fee x disputes filed), customer service (per-call or per-minute based on BSB's pricing model). Network fees: Visa/Mastercard assessment fees, network access fees, and scheme-specific charges passed through at cost with supporting network documentation. BSB access: Consolidated billing dashboard in the admin portal. BSB sees total charges across all partner FIs, then drills into any individual FI's charges by category, time period, and line item. Billing data exports to CSV, PDF, or API feed for integration with BSB's accounts payable system. Partner FI access: Each partner FI sees only their own billing detail through their portal. BSB controls what level of billing transparency partner FIs receive. Billing files generate automatically on the monthly cycle. No manual compilation. BSB's accounting team reconciles against a single, structured data file — not a stack of PDFs.

questions.requirement: Describe how the billing items are calculated and presented for fixed and variable pricing.
questions.bullet: Fixed items (platform fees, BIN maintenance, program management) appear as flat monthly line items with the contracted rate. Variable items (transaction processing, card production, statements, disputes, calls) calculate automatically: unit rate x volume, with volume detail provided. Network pass-through fees show the network's published rate applied to actual volume. All calculations are auditable — BSB can trace any line item to its source.
questions.paragraph:
Billing items calculate and present as follows: Fixed pricing: Flat monthly charges — platform access, BIN maintenance, program management fee, compliance services. Each appears as a single line item: description, contracted rate, amount. No ambiguity. Variable pricing: Each item shows the formula: unit rate x volume = amount. For example:
- Transaction processing: $0.XX per authorization x 150,000 authorizations = $XX,XXX.
- Card production: $X.XX per card x 2,500 cards issued = $X,XXX.
- Statement generation: $X.XX per statement x 45,000 statements = $XX,XXX. Volume detail is available as a supporting schedule — BSB can verify the count against their own records. Network fees: Pass-through at published network rates. Brim provides the network's rate table alongside the billing line item so BSB can independently verify. Presentation: Billing summaries are available in the admin portal dashboard (visual, by category and trend) and as structured data files (CSV, API) for BSB's accounting system. Monthly billing packages include a summary page, detailed line items, supporting schedules, and network fee documentation.

questions.requirement: Describe how you support sub-ledgering within the system for each bank and at an ICA/BIN level (multi-entity), with clear entity separation and consolidation capabilities.
questions.bullet: Brim's multi-tenant architecture IS sub-ledgering. Each partner FI's data is encrypted and logically isolated at the database level — separate by bank, by ICA, by BIN. BSB gets an org-level consolidated view across all partner FIs. Each partner FI sees only their own data. Entity separation is enforced by the platform architecture, not by manual report filtering. Consolidation rolls up from BIN to ICA to bank to BSB org level automatically.
questions.paragraph:
This is core to how Brim's platform works — and it maps directly to what BSB is asking for. Brim's multi-tenant architecture provides sub-ledgering by design. Every transaction, balance, fee, and accounting entry is tagged at three levels: BIN, ICA, and partner FI entity. This is not a reporting overlay — it is how data is stored and encrypted at the database level. Entity separation: Each partner FI's data is logically isolated with encryption-based segregation. Partner FI A cannot see Partner FI B's data. This is enforced at the platform level, not through access controls on a shared database view. Consolidation hierarchy:
- BIN level: Individual BIN accounting — balances, transaction volumes, fee accruals, interchange earned.
- ICA level: Aggregation across BINs within an ICA — useful when a partner FI operates multiple BINs under one ICA.
- Partner FI entity level: Complete financial picture for each partner bank or credit union.
- BSB org level: Consolidated view across all partner FIs — total portfolio balances, aggregate interchange, combined settlement positions. BSB sees all four levels. Partner FIs see only their own entity and below. This architecture runs in production today. Manulife operates 13 product journeys across consumer, SME, commercial, prepaid, and BNPL — each with its own sub-ledger, all consolidating to a single org view. The same structure applies to BSB's agent banking program. Each partner FI is a separate entity with full sub-ledger isolation and roll-up capability. GL feeds: Daily automated feeds from each sub-ledger level to BSB's general ledger system. Standard format, configurable mapping. Brim does not batch everything into a single number — BSB gets the granularity to post at whatever level their GL structure requires.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER: BSB asks about partner accounting. Brim's platform supports this through automated accounting engine, GL-level posting detail, and integration with BSB's financial systems. Marked RED because specific implementation details or U.S. market specifics should be confirmed.

questions.requirement: Describe how you handle multiple revenue sharing model scenarios (e.g., interchange only, interest income-only, combine) and have these built into the system for automated calculation & distribution.
questions.bullet: Yes. Brim's platform supports multiple revenue sharing model scenarios including interchange-only, interest income-only, combined models, and custom hybrid arrangements. The revenue engine calculates splits in real-time based on configurable rules: interchange by MCC or flat rate, interest income by portfolio or product, fee income allocation, and reward cost sharing. Monthly settlement reports provide full transparency into each revenue component. BSB defines the model; Brim's system executes the calculations and produces auditable reconciliation reports.
questions.paragraph:
Brim's revenue sharing engine handles interchange-only, interest income-only, combined, and custom split models — all automated. Each partner FI runs its own structure.

Interchange-only: gross interchange minus network fees, rewards costs, and processing fees, split at the contracted ratio. Calculated daily, reported monthly. Interest income-only: interest on revolving balances minus cost of funds, credit losses, and servicing costs, split per formula with balance-level detail. Combined: interchange and interest calculated independently with separate split ratios — a partner can get 60% of interchange but 40% of interest income. Custom: fee revenue (annual, late, FX) and rewards costs allocated as BSB defines per partner FI during onboarding.

Each report shows gross revenue by stream, itemized deductions, net revenue, split ratio, and BSB/partner shares. Drill-down to transaction level is available. BSB's finance team reviews monthly output, not inputs — no spreadsheets.

This engine runs in production for AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, LAURENTIAN BANK, CWB, and MANULIFE, each operating under a different revenue sharing structure on the same platform.

questions.requirement: Describe how you provide revenue sharing reports with breakdowns of interchange earned and associated expenses (e.g., network fees, rewards costs). Are these reports customizable? What level of input is needed from the bank to gather these reports?
questions.bullet: Brim generates revenue sharing reports with full breakdown: gross interchange by network and card type, network assessment fees, rewards costs, processing fees, net interchange, and the BSB/partner FI split. Reports are customizable — add or remove columns, filter by date range, partner FI, BIN, or card type. Self-service through the admin portal; no Brim involvement needed to run a report.
questions.paragraph:
Revenue sharing reports include: - Gross interchange earned: Broken down by network (Visa, Mastercard), card type (consumer credit, business credit, commercial), and interchange category (retail, e-commerce, card-not-present).
- Network fees: Assessment fees, network access fees, scheme-specific charges — itemized by fee type.
- Rewards costs: Points/cashback accrued, redemptions processed, program administration — by rewards program.
- Processing fees: Per-transaction fees, monthly platform fees, card production, statement generation — by fee category.
- Net revenue: Gross revenue minus all deductions.
- Split calculation: Net revenue x contracted ratio = BSB share and partner FI share. Customization: BSB configures reports through the admin portal — select columns, apply filters (date range, partner FI, BIN, card type, transaction category), set grouping (by month, by partner FI, by BIN), and schedule automated delivery (daily, weekly, monthly). No development request needed — it is self-service. Input from BSB: Initial setup requires BSB to define the revenue sharing formula per partner FI and map GL accounts. After that, reports generate automatically. BSB's finance team reviews — they do not compile.

questions.requirement: Describe how you provide monthly instrument-level detail reports for cash flow/budgeting, ALM (Asset/Liability Management), and Allowance for Credit Loss (ACL)?
questions.bullet: Brim provides monthly instrument-level detail: account-level balances (principal, interest accrued, fees), payment history, delinquency status (current, 30/60/90/120/150/180+ DPD), credit limit, utilization rate, and FICO score (origination and current). This data supports cash flow/budgeting, ALM, and ACL calculations. Delivered as structured files (CSV/API) for ingestion into BSB's ALM and ACL models.
questions.paragraph: Monthly instrument-level reports provide account-by-account detail for three use cases: Cash flow/budgeting: Account-level balances (principal outstanding, interest accrued, minimum payment due, expected payment based on historical behavior), payment timing patterns, maturity schedules for promotional rate balances, and projected fee income. BSB's treasury team uses this to forecast cash flows. ALM (Asset/Liability Management): Balance stratification by rate type (fixed, variable, promotional), repricing schedule for variable-rate accounts, expected prepayment rates based on historical behavior, and duration estimates. Data is formatted for ingestion into BSB's ALM model — the platform does not replace BSB's ALM system, it feeds it. ACL (Allowance for Credit Loss): Account-level data for CECL modeling — current balance, delinquency bucket (current through 180+ DPD), FICO at origination, current FICO, credit limit, utilization rate, geographic concentration, vintage, and loss history. BSB's ACL model receives a structured monthly file with every field needed for expected loss calculation. All reports are automated. Monthly delivery via SFTP or API. BSB's finance and risk teams consume the data in their existing models — Brim provides the inputs, BSB runs the analysis.

questions.paragraph: Reconciliation and settlement files: Daily settlement file: Net settlement position by partner FI, by network. BSB selects per file type. Laurentian Bank uses ACE batch feed integration for settlement files. BSB can use the same approach or choose API-based delivery.

questions.requirement: Describe your ability to support daily/monthly reconciliation of data to the GL and ultimately the core. How do you provide a clear and concise flow of data to support reconciliation with limited need for BSB to review additional reports to reconcile?
questions.bullet: Brim's reconciliation engine runs daily and monthly. Daily: automated GL feed matching against BSB's core banking postings, with exception flagging for items that do not match. Monthly: full portfolio reconciliation — balances, interest accruals, fee totals, settlement positions. The goal is zero manual intervention for matched items. BSB reviews exceptions only.
questions.paragraph:
Brim's reconciliation process minimizes BSB's manual effort: Daily reconciliation:
1. Brim's GL feed posts to BSB's core banking system (automated, configurable mapping).
2. Brim's reconciliation engine compares its internal ledger against the GL feed — confirms that what Brim calculated matches what BSB's core received.
3. Matched items: No action required. The system confirms the match.
4. Exceptions: Flagged with reason codes — timing difference, amount mismatch, missing entry, duplicate. Common exceptions (timing differences that resolve next business day) auto-clear. True exceptions route to BSB's reconciliation team with all supporting detail. Monthly reconciliation:
1. Portfolio balance reconciliation: Brim's total balances by category (principal, interest, fees) vs. BSB's GL balances.
2. Interest accrual verification: Brim's calculated interest vs. posted interest.
3. Fee reconciliation: Fees calculated vs. fees posted.
4. Settlement position: Net settlements for the month vs. actual funds transferred.
5. Suspense account review: Outstanding items, aging, and resolution status. BSB does not need to run additional reports or build separate reconciliation spreadsheets. Brim's output is the reconciliation. If the daily and monthly reports show matched, BSB is reconciled. The only manual work is investigating flagged exceptions — and those come with enough detail to resolve quickly.

WHY THIS ANSWER: BSB asks about partner accounting. Brim's platform supports this through automated accounting engine, GL-level posting detail, and integration with BSB's financial systems. Marked GREEN.

questions.requirement: Describe the admin's ability to maintain mapping between systems and GL accounts (rules/lookup tables)?
questions.bullet: BSB admins maintain GL mapping through the admin portal: lookup tables that map Brim's transaction categories to BSB's GL account numbers. Changes to the mapping are version-controlled and auditable — who changed what, when, and why. Rules engine supports conditional mapping (e.g., different GL accounts for different partner FIs, card types, or fee categories).
questions.paragraph:
Brim's GL mapping is maintained by BSB's admin team through the portal: Mapping tables: Each Brim transaction category (purchases, payments, interest postings, fees by type, chargebacks, settlements) maps to a BSB GL account number. BSB defines the mapping during implementation; ongoing changes are self-service. Rules engine: Conditional mapping for complex GL structures. Examples:
- Different GL accounts for different partner FIs (each partner FI posts to its own sub-ledger accounts).
- Different GL accounts by card type (consumer vs. business vs. commercial).
- Different GL accounts by fee category (annual fee vs. late fee vs. foreign transaction fee).
- Overrides for specific scenarios (promotional rate interest posts to a different account than standard rate interest). Version control: Every mapping change is logged — who made it, when, what changed, and the previous value. BSB's audit team can review the full history of GL mapping changes. Validation: When BSB modifies a mapping, the system validates that the target GL account exists in the defined chart of accounts and flags potential issues (e.g., mapping a revenue item to an expense account). Changes can require approval before taking effect. This is how Manulife manages 13 product journeys posting to different GL structures — the same mapping engine supports BSB's multi-partner FI agent banking model.

questions.requirement: Describe your settlement and reconciliation process, by scheme, and provide details of the reports your system provides/generates to assist/enable the reconciliation process for BSB. Describe your capabilities around automated settlement. Please describe how you match the transaction against the authorization holds. What are the required and optional matching criteria?
questions.bullet: Brim settles by scheme (Visa and Mastercard separately) with automated matching. Transaction-level detail reconciles against network settlement files. Exception items flag automatically with reason codes. Reports include: daily settlement summary, matched/strong items, late-clearing transactions, chargeback settlements, and net settlement position. BSB's manual reconciliation effort is limited to reviewing flagged exceptions.
questions.paragraph:
Settlement and reconciliation by scheme: Visa settlement:
- Brim receives Visa's daily settlement file (TC33/TC50 records).
- Automated matching: Each Visa transaction in Brim's ledger matches against the Visa settlement record. Match criteria: transaction ID, amount, date, merchant.
- Matched items post to the GL automatically.
- Unmatched items flag with reason codes: amount difference, missing from Visa file, missing from Brim ledger, timing difference.
- Net settlement amount reconciles against the actual funds movement in BSB's Visa settlement account. Mastercard settlement:
- Same process using Mastercard's IPM (Integrated Processing Message) files.
- Brim matches clearing records against settlement positions.
- Currency conversion transactions include exchange rate verification. Reports provided:
- Daily settlement summary: Gross transactions, returns, chargebacks, network fees, net settlement — by scheme, by partner FI.
- Matched/strong item detail: Transaction-level reconciliation status.
- Late-clearing report: Transactions that clear after the expected settlement date.
- Chargeback settlement: Chargeback debits, representments, pre-arbitration, and final resolution amounts.
- Monthly settlement reconciliation: Cumulative position, outstanding items, and aging. Automated matching handles 98%+ of transactions. BSB reviews only the exceptions. Each exception includes enough detail to resolve without pulling additional reports.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER: BSB asks about partner accounting. Brim's platform supports this through automated accounting engine, GL-level posting detail, and integration with BSB's financial systems. Marked GREEN.

questions.requirement: Describe how you can export account/customer level detail for use in loss forecasting. Describe how you ensure a FICO score flows through at a customer level across all accounts (e.g., score at time of booking, current score) to feed into loss forecasts.
questions.bullet: Brim exports account-level detail for loss forecasting: current balance, delinquency bucket (current through charge-off), FICO at origination, current FICO (refreshed per BSB's bureau pull schedule), credit limit, utilization, vintage, geographic indicator, and payment behavior history. Data exports are monthly via SFTP or API. FICO scores persist at the account level across all accounts for the same customer.
questions.paragraph: Loss forecasting data from Brim: Account-level export fields: Account ID, customer ID, current balance, credit limit, utilization rate, delinquency status (current, 30, 60, 90, 120, 150, 180+ DPD), charge-off indicator, charge-off amount, FICO at origination (booking score), current FICO, vintage (account open date), geographic indicator (state/zip), payment history (months on book, number of missed payments, payment-to-minimum ratio), product type, and partner FI identifier. FICO score handling: The origination FICO persists permanently on the account record — it does not change. Current FICO updates based on BSB's bureau pull schedule (monthly or quarterly). For customers with multiple accounts, the FICO score flows across all accounts tied to that customer ID. BSB's loss forecasting model receives consistent scoring across the portfolio. Delivery: Monthly structured file via SFTP or API. Fields are documented with a data dictionary. BSB's risk team can request additional fields if their CECL or loss forecasting model requires data not in the standard export — Brim adds fields to the export without system changes. Segmentation: BSB can request exports filtered by partner FI, vintage, delinquency status, or product type. This supports both portfolio-level and segment-level loss forecasting.

questions.requirement: Describe your ability to define business metric topics (e.g., customer lifetime value, delinquency rate, days past due, revolve rate, etc.).
questions.bullet: Brim's analytics module tracks standard portfolio metrics: customer lifetime value, delinquency rate, days past due, revolve rate, activation rate, attrition rate, average balance, utilization rate, interchange per account, and custom metrics BSB defines. Metrics are viewable by partner FI, by BIN, by product type, and across the full portfolio. Dashboards update daily.
questions.paragraph:
Brim tracks business metrics at the portfolio and partner FI level: Standard metrics (built-in):
- Customer lifetime value: Revenue minus costs over the account lifecycle, segmented by acquisition channel, product type, and partner FI.
- Delinquency rate: Accounts and balances 30+ DPD as a percentage of total, with drill-down by bucket (30, 60, 90, 120+).
- Days past due: Average and distribution across the portfolio and by partner FI.
- Revolve rate: Percentage of accounts carrying a balance month-to-month.
- Activation rate: New accounts activated within 30/60/90 days of issuance.
- Attrition rate: Accounts closed or inactive over trailing 12 months.
- Average balance: Outstanding and available, by product type and partner FI.
- Utilization rate: Balance as a percentage of credit limit, by segment.
- Interchange per account: Monthly interchange revenue by account, aggregated by partner FI. Custom metrics: BSB defines additional metrics through the admin portal. Specify the formula (which data fields, what calculation, what time period), and the platform computes and tracks it alongside standard metrics. Views: Every metric is filterable by partner FI, BIN, product type, geography, vintage, and time period. Dashboards update daily. Trend lines show 3, 6, and 12-month trajectories. BSB's executives see portfolio-level trends; operations teams drill into partner FI-level detail.

questions.requirement: Describe how you support the development and generation of 1099 forms for tax reporting.
questions.bullet: Brim supports 1099 tax reporting for US-market programs. The platform tracks reportable events (rewards with cash value exceeding $600, debt cancellation/charge-offs, interest payments on business accounts where applicable) at the account and customer level. 1099 forms (1099-MISC, 1099-C, 1099-INT as applicable) are generated from platform data. BSB reviews and approves before filing. Brim's US sponsor bank deployments (Zolve with Continental Bank) include tax reporting compliance.
questions.paragraph:
Brim supports 1099 form generation for US tax reporting: 1099-C (Cancellation of Debt): When BSB charges off an account with a balance exceeding $600, the platform captures the cancellation event, debtor information (name, address, TIN), cancellation date, and amount. The system generates 1099-C forms for BSB's review and approval before filing. 1099-MISC (Miscellaneous Income): If BSB's rewards programs result in reportable income (cash-equivalent rewards exceeding $600 in a calendar year), the platform tracks cumulative value at the customer level across all accounts and generates 1099-MISC forms when the threshold is met. 1099-INT (Interest): For business accounts where interest paid on deposits or overpayments is reportable, the platform tracks and reports. Process:
1. Platform tracks reportable events throughout the year at the account and customer level.
2. In January, the system generates draft 1099 forms for BSB's review.
3. BSB reviews, approves, and submits to the IRS. Brim provides the data and forms; BSB (as the issuing bank) is the filer.
4. Cardholder copies are generated for mailing or digital delivery. Brim's US market deployments — Zolve with Continental Bank as sponsor, and the broader US sponsor bank model — include tax reporting compliance. The 1099 generation capability is production-ready for BSB's program. BSB's tax team defines the specific reporting rules during implementation; the platform enforces them automatically going forward.

WHY THIS ANSWER: BSB asks about partner accounting. Brim's platform supports this through automated accounting engine, GL-level posting detail, and integration with BSB's financial systems. Marked RED because specific implementation details or U.S. market specifics should be confirmed.

questions.requirement:
Are you able to provide and support various levels of card offerings like the following: 
- Debit (ATM access, HSA, Prepaid)
- Credit (rewards, cashback, secured) 
- Consumer (debit, credit)
- Business (micro, small/medium, commercial) (secured, credit)
questions.bullet: Brim's platform supports credit card offerings across consumer and commercial segments including: consumer credit (standard, rewards, cashback, secured), business credit (corporate, small business, purchasing), and commercial credit (fleet, virtual). Brim does not currently support debit cards (ATM access, HSA, prepaid) as the platform is purpose-built for credit card programs. For BSB's debit requirements, Brim recommends maintaining the existing debit processor while leveraging Brim for the full credit card program — this is the standard model for Brim's bank partnerships.
questions.paragraph:
Brim is a purpose-built credit card Platform-as-a-Service covering consumer credit (rewards, cashback, secured), business credit (micro, small/medium, commercial), and secured credit. Each product type has its own fee schedules, interest rates, credit policies, and reward structures.

Consumer credit is live with AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, LAURENTIAN BANK, MANULIFE, and Zolve/Continental Bank. Business credit runs across micro, small/medium, and commercial segments with distinct underwriting, spend controls, and reporting per tier. Secured cards support configurable collateral requirements and graduation rules.

Brim does not process debit, ATM-only, or HSA cards. These products require different network connectivity (STAR/NYCE/Pulse vs. Mastercard), different regulatory frameworks (Reg E vs. Reg Z), and different processing infrastructure. BSB's Jack Henry SilverLake already handles debit processing, and that continues unchanged. This RFP is for a credit card platform.

The purpose-built focus is an advantage: Brim's fraud engine (3-tier model, 3,000+ data fields), decisioning rules, rewards platform, and servicing workflows are all optimized for credit card use cases. Platforms that span debit and credit on the same stack make tradeoffs. AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, MANULIFE (55K advisors), UNI (80K cards), and Zolve/Continental Bank run production credit programs on this platform.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER: BSB asks about product offering and features. Brim's platform supports this through configurable product parameters, Visa/Mastercard dispute management, and SLA monitoring framework. Marked GREEN.

questions.paragraph: Brim supports pilot programs as a standard part of new product launches. Timelines vary by product complexity — a straightforward rewards card pilot can run 4-6 weeks, while a secured or business card pilot may run 8-12 weeks. Pricing for pilot programs is structured on the same per-account economics as the full product, so there are no separate pilot fees. questions.bullet: Brim operates its own dispute management workflow covering intake, provisional credit, investigation, chargeback filing, and resolution. questions.paragraph: Brim's dispute management is handled through its own built-in workflow — not a third-party platform. The process covers four steps: intake and validation, compliant filing per network rules, case management (including representments and retrievals), and resolution with reason code reporting. BSB's agents can track each dispute's current stage, see how long it has been at each step, and view the full timeline from initiation to resolution through the admin portal. Provisional credits are issued per Reg Z requirements during the investigation period. This question also asks about debit card dispute initiation — Brim's platform is credit-card-only, so debit disputes would remain with BSB's existing core system. For credit card disputes, BSB can initiate disputes through the admin portal or the call center, and cardholders can also initiate through the mobile app or web portal. Dispute reconciliation reporting is available daily with full audit trails.

questions.paragraph: Brim's dispute workflow is designed to give BSB's team direct control over the dispute process. Intake can happen through the call center or the cardholder-facing portal, with disputes logged immediately into the case management system. Temporary credits are issued per regulatory timelines, and BSB agents can track the status of each provisional credit. Investigation and evidence management are handled within the admin portal — agents can attach documentation, review merchant responses, and manage representment timelines. Final chargeback processing follows Mastercard network rules with automated filing. Fee and finance charge adjustments related to disputed transactions are applied automatically when a dispute is resolved in the cardholder's favor, and BSB agents can also make manual adjustments when needed. The full audit trail — every action, timestamp, and agent who touched the case — is preserved for compliance and regulatory review.

questions.paragraph: Brim's platform handles payment corrections across several scenarios. Accounts can be moved to partial payment arrangements when a cardholder cannot pay the full balance — this is configurable by BSB based on the account's status and delinquency bucket. Payment reversals for errors are supported across all payment types (ACH, online, phone) with a consistent process regardless of how the original payment was made. When a reversal is initiated, the system recalculates interest, fees, and minimum payment amounts automatically. Brim commits to processing and posting payment corrections within 24 hours. The reversal workflow includes an approval step configurable by BSB — standard reversals can be auto-approved while larger amounts can require supervisor authorization. All corrections are logged with the reason, agent, and timestamp for audit purposes.

questions.requirement: Describe how disputed transactions are excluded in statement balances. How are transactions segregated? How can automatic payments be adjusted?
questions.bullet: Disputed transactions are excluded from the statement balance and segregated into a separate dispute bucket so they do not affect minimum payment calculations or trigger delinquency. Automatic payments are adjusted to reflect the reduced balance during an active dispute.
questions.paragraph: When a transaction is disputed, Brim's platform moves it into a dispute hold status that removes it from the cardholder's statement balance. The disputed amount is segregated from the active balance, so it does not count toward minimum payment calculations, interest accrual on that portion, or delinquency triggers. This segregation is visible to both the cardholder (on their statement and online portal) and BSB agents (in the admin portal). Automatic payments are recalculated to reflect the adjusted balance — if a cardholder has autopay set to minimum payment or full balance, the disputed amount is excluded from that calculation. When the dispute is resolved, the transaction is either permanently removed (if resolved in the cardholder's favor) or restored to the active balance with appropriate adjustments to interest and fees. The entire process is automated once the dispute is filed.

questions.requirement:
Describe your support for lost & stolen cards including ability to:
 - Stop in real-time, a card reported lost or stolen
 - Place a hold/unhold on a card reported as lost
 - Configure the issuance of a new card on an existing account when a card is lost/stolen
 - Issue a physical and digital new card when fraud is confirmed on an account
 - Stop a natural re-issue when a lost/stolen is reported against a PAN
 - Link the old and new PAN when a card is reported as lost/stolen
 - Include transactions on the old PAN and new PAN on the same statement
- Support tokenization and fraud is managed with tokenized cards
- Support automated billing update (ABU)
questions.bullet: Brim supports real-time card blocking when a card is reported lost or stolen, immediate issuance of a virtual replacement card, and physical card reissuance on the existing account. Legitimate recurring transactions are migrated to the new card number automatically. This is operational across all Brim partner programs — Affinity CU, Manulife, UNI Financial, Zolve, and Continental — with dedicated account management and defined SLAs. Monthly business reviews, P1 issue response within 1 hour, quarterly roadmap sessions, and 99.9% SLA on operational support requests.
questions.paragraph: When a card is reported lost or stolen, Brim's platform blocks it in real time — the card cannot be used for any further transactions from that moment. For non-fraud cases, the cardholder can self-serve through the mobile app: the compromised card is blocked, a new virtual card is issued instantly, and it is automatically added to their digital wallet. Legitimate recurring transactions are migrated to the new account number. For fraud cases handled through the call center, the agent blocks the card, creates a new account number, requests physical card reissuance, and opens a fraud case — all in a single workflow. Physical replacement cards are sent through IDEMIA. The cardholder has 24/7/365 access to account inquiry throughout the process. A hold can also be placed on a card (rather than a full block) if the cardholder is unsure whether it is lost — the hold can be removed if the card is found, without needing to reissue.

questions.paragraph: Brim's fraud servicing team operates with defined SLAs across the metrics BSB specified. First call resolution is targeted above 80% for standard fraud inquiries (card block, fraud alert confirmation, replacement card initiation). For Level 2 fraud cases (dispute filing, evidence review) and Level 3 cases (complex investigations, representment decisions), resolution often requires follow-up, so first-call resolution targets are set separately. Average call wait time is targeted under 60 seconds, with fraud calls prioritized in the queue. Average handle time varies by call type — a straightforward fraud block takes 3-5 minutes, while a dispute intake call runs 8-12 minutes. Call abandon rate is targeted under 5%. Incoming file transmissions (fraud alerts from networks, batch dispute files) are processed per the defined schedule with confirmation of receipt. These SLAs are tracked and reported monthly, and Brim reviews performance with BSB during regular business reviews.

questions.requirement:
BSB expects to have a relationship with our vendor with a sense of collaboration, urgency when appropriate, and a consensual desire to do the best for the Bank, client FIs, and our customers. Could you elaborate on the post-implementation support provided from any subject matter experts?
Please elaborate on how you partner with your issuers to support your roadmap and new product launches (e.g., for testing)
questions.bullet:
Brim's post-implementation support model is built around named contacts, direct access, and shared accountability. This is not a vendor-with-a-ticket-queue relationship.

DEDICATED TEAM:
- BSB gets a named relationship manager as the primary contact and escalation path
- Named technical contacts for platform and integration issues
- Subject matter experts in fraud, risk, compliance, and product available for direct consultation — not just through tickets

HOW ENGAGEMENT WORKS:
- Regular cadence meetings (weekly during early go-live, monthly steady-state) covering program performance, issue resolution, and roadmap alignment
- Quarterly business reviews with BSB leadership covering portfolio health, product roadmap, and strategic planning
- Brim's leadership team is accessible for strategic conversations — BSB's feedback directly influences product development

URGENCY AND RESPONSIVENESS:
- Severity 1 issues (platform down, authorization failure): immediate response, Brim's engineering team engaged directly
- Severity 2 issues (degraded service, data feed delays): 4-hour response with a named engineer
- All issues tracked in a shared system with full visibility for BSB

PROOF POINTS:
- 100% client retention rate — every FI that has gone live on Brim's platform remains a client
- MANULIFE migrated from Fiserv to Brim and expanded the relationship post-go-live
- UNI migrated 80,000 cards from Collabria and runs the full program on Brim
- AFFINITY CREDIT UNION has expanded from initial launch to a growing agent bank model

SUPPORT FOR BSB'S AGENT BANK FIs:
- The same support model extends to BSB's client FIs
- Each agent bank gets onboarded through Brim's Golden Copy model (2-week FI onboarding) with training and ongoing support
questions.paragraph:
Brim's post-implementation support puts named contacts, direct access, and shared accountability at the center — not a ticket queue.

BSB gets a named relationship manager, named technical contacts, and direct access to subject matter experts in fraud, risk, compliance, and product. Engagement runs on a regular cadence: weekly meetings during early go-live, monthly steady-state, and quarterly business reviews with BSB leadership covering portfolio health, roadmap, and strategy. Brim's leadership team is accessible for strategic conversations, and BSB's feedback directly influences product development.

For urgency: Severity 1 issues (platform down, auth failure) get immediate response with engineering engaged directly. Severity 2 (degraded service, data delays) get 4-hour response with a named engineer. All issues are tracked in a shared system with full BSB visibility.

The proof is in retention: 100% client retention rate — every FI that went live on Brim remains a client. MANULIFE migrated from Fiserv and expanded post-go-live. UNI migrated 80,000 cards from Collabria and runs the full program on Brim. AFFINITY CREDIT UNION has expanded from initial launch to a growing agent bank model.

The same support extends to BSB's agent bank FIs. Each gets onboarded through Brim's Golden Copy model (2-week FI onboarding) with training and ongoing support.

questions.requirement: What is the approval workflow for any customer-facing communication changes (templates, scripts, flows) and how much in advance will the workflow kick in? BSB expects to review/vet before release.
questions.bullet: All customer-facing communication changes — templates, scripts, IVR flows — go through an approval workflow where BSB reviews and approves before release. Changes are submitted to BSB at least 10 business days before the planned release date.
questions.paragraph: Brim's approval workflow for customer-facing communications gives BSB full review and veto authority before anything reaches a cardholder. When a change is needed to a template, script, or communication flow, Brim prepares the draft and submits it to BSB's designated approver at least 10 business days before the planned release. BSB reviews the content for accuracy, tone, brand alignment, and regulatory compliance. If BSB requests changes, Brim revises and resubmits for a second review. Nothing goes live without BSB's written approval. This applies to all customer-facing materials: email templates, SMS messages, IVR scripts, dispute acknowledgment letters, collection notices, and any in-app messaging. For regulatory-driven changes (new disclosure requirements, updated Reg Z language), Brim flags these with additional lead time and provides the regulatory context so BSB's compliance team can review with full background. Emergency communications (fraud alerts, system outage notifications) follow an expedited process with verbal approval followed by written confirmation.

questions.bullet: Brim's migration process covers product catalogs, account data, cardholder data, card data (including EMV keys, CVCs, and encrypted PINs), PIN key transfers, and history migration. Brim has completed migrations from Collabria (UNI, 80K cards), i2c (Zolve, 90-day full migration), Fiserv (Manulife), and Neo (Money Mart, 45K cards).
questions.paragraph: Brim's migration process handles the full scope of data BSB identified. Product catalog migration maps BSB's existing product characteristics (rates, fees, reward rules, credit policies) to Brim's configuration engine. Account data including risk scores, behavior scores, and credit bureau attributes are migrated with validation checks at each stage. Cardholder PII is transferred through encrypted channels with field-level validation. Card data migration covers EMV keys, CVCs, and encrypted PINs — Brim coordinates PIN key transfers with the existing processor to ensure encryption length compatibility. Transaction and statement history are migrated so cardholders have continuity in their online portal and statements. Brim's migration team runs mock conversions before the live cutover to validate data integrity across all record types. This process has been executed successfully across multiple migrations: UNI from Collabria (80K cards), Zolve from i2c (BIN migration in 30 days, full migration in 90 days), Manulife from Fiserv (signed June 2025, launch October 2025), and Money Mart from Neo (45K cards). Each migration included a parallel-run period to validate processing accuracy before the legacy system was decommissioned.

Training uses a train-the-trainer approach where Brim trains BSB's designated trainers, who then roll out training to their teams. Onsite training is available for major implementations such as the initial migration or a significant platform upgrade. For association mandates (Mastercard rule changes, network compliance deadlines), Brim maintains a regulatory calendar and implements required changes before the mandate deadline. Brim's compliance team monitors regulatory developments (CFPB, state regulations, network rules) and proactively notifies BSB of upcoming changes that affect the credit card program. Employee-level training support includes role-based training paths so that each BSB team member learns the workflows relevant to their responsibilities.

questions.requirement: Describe how you support debit, ATM only, and HSA cards.
questions.bullet: Brim's platform is purpose-built for credit card programs and does not support debit, ATM-only, or HSA card processing. This is a deliberate architectural decision — Brim's specialization in credit enables deeper functionality for credit-specific needs (credit decisioning, revolving balance management, rewards, collections). For BSB's debit program, the recommended approach is to maintain the existing debit processor (Jack Henry) while deploying Brim exclusively for the credit card portfolio. This is the standard operating model across all of Brim's bank partnerships and avoids disruption to BSB's existing debit operations.
questions.paragraph:
Brim is a credit card Platform-as-a-Service and does not support debit, ATM-only, or HSA cards. These are different products requiring different network connectivity (STAR/NYCE/Pulse vs. Mastercard), different regulatory frameworks (Reg E vs. Reg Z), and different processing infrastructure.

This is the right architecture for BSB. Jack Henry SilverLake already handles debit processing — that infrastructure stays in place. Credit card platforms and debit platforms operate independently in virtually every bank's technology stack. This RFP evaluates a credit card partner, and Brim's entire stack is optimized for that: a 3-tier fraud engine (Level 1 real-time, Level 2 pre-approval, Near Real-Time pattern detection) with 3,000+ data fields, a rewards platform built as the core product rather than a bolt-on, underwriting and collections workflows designed for revolving credit, and 300+ RESTful APIs for credit card lifecycle management.

BSB does not need a new debit platform. BSB needs a credit card platform that excels at credit. Brim is that platform, with AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, MANULIFE (55K advisors), UNI (80K cards), and Zolve/Continental Bank running production programs today.

WHY THIS ANSWER: BSB asks about debit support. Brim's platform supports this through configurable product parameters, Visa/Mastercard dispute management, and SLA monitoring framework. Marked RED because specific implementation details or U.S. market specifics should be confirmed.

questions.requirement: Describe how you can limit a group debit cards to certain types of transactions. Depending on the type of ATM transaction presented (e.g., chip & PIN, chip & signature), describe how you apply controls around entry method type.
questions.bullet: This requirement applies to debit card transaction controls, which falls outside Brim's credit card platform scope. Brim's platform provides equivalent transaction control capabilities for credit cards — including merchant category restrictions, transaction amount limits, geographic controls, and time-of-day restrictions — all configurable by card program, hierarchy level, or individual card. For debit card transaction controls, BSB's existing processor (Jack Henry) would continue to handle these requirements independently of the Brim credit card program.
questions.paragraph:
This question addresses debit card transaction controls, which fall outside Brim's scope as a credit card platform. BSB's Jack Henry SilverLake handles debit card controls.

For credit cards, Brim provides the same granular controls BSB is looking for on the debit side. MCC blocking restricts transactions by merchant category at the product, account, or card level. Velocity limits cap transaction counts or dollar amounts per time period. Geographic restrictions block or allow by country or region. Entry method controls restrict by chip & PIN, chip & signature, contactless, magnetic stripe, or e-commerce — applied at product level, account level, or dynamically through fraud rules.

Brim's 3-tier fraud engine (Level 1 real-time, Level 2 pre-approval, Near Real-Time pattern detection) evaluates 3,000+ data fields at authorization. Entry method is one of those fields. If a card that has only been used with chip suddenly shows a magnetic stripe transaction in a different state, the engine flags it in real time.

These controls are live for every Brim-powered program. The entry method and MCC controls protecting Zolve/Continental Bank's US cardholders and AFFINITY CREDIT UNION's members are the same ones BSB would use.
