questions.requirement: Can you describe or provide information about your information security program?
questions.bullet: Brim maintains a mature information security program validated by three independent certifications: PCI DSS v4.0.1, SOC 2 Type 2 (audited by Coalfire), and ISO 27001. The program covers data encryption, access controls, vulnerability management, incident response, and continuous monitoring.
questions.paragraph: Brim's information security program is built around three independently validated frameworks. PCI DSS v4.0.1 certification covers cardholder data protection, network security, access controls, and regular testing. SOC 2 Type 2 (audited by Coalfire) validates controls over security, availability, and processing integrity across the production environment. ISO 27001 certification confirms that Brim operates a formal information security management system with documented policies, risk assessments, and continuous improvement processes. The program includes encryption of data at rest and in transit, role-based access controls with least-privilege principles, regular vulnerability scanning and penetration testing, an incident response plan with defined escalation paths, and continuous monitoring of the production environment. Brim partners with Imperva for bot detection and credential stuffing defense at the application layer. The platform is hosted at Leaseweb Montreal with DDoS protection providing 10-second mitigation and 5 TB/s capacity. Security policies are reviewed and updated annually, and Brim's security team tracks emerging threats to adjust controls proactively.

questions.requirement: Can you describe or provide information about your privacy program?
questions.bullet: Brim maintains a privacy program covering data collection, use, retention, and disclosure practices. The program aligns with applicable privacy regulations and is documented in Brim's consumer privacy notice, which is available for BSB's review.
questions.paragraph: Brim's privacy program governs how cardholder data is collected, used, stored, and shared. The program defines what personal information is collected during account origination and servicing, the purposes for which it is used, how long it is retained, and the conditions under which it may be disclosed to third parties. Brim's privacy practices align with applicable privacy regulations in the jurisdictions where it operates. Data minimization principles guide what information is collected — only data necessary for account origination, servicing, fraud prevention, and regulatory compliance is captured. Cardholder data is stored in Brim's secure environment at Leaseweb Montreal, subject to the access controls and encryption standards covered under Brim's PCI DSS and SOC 2 certifications. Brim's consumer privacy notice is available for BSB's review and can be customized to reflect BSB's branding and any additional privacy commitments BSB makes to its customers. The privacy program is reviewed annually and updated as regulations evolve.
questions.rationale:
WHY THIS ANSWER: BSB asks about privacy/information security. Brim's platform supports this through configurable product parameters, Visa/Mastercard dispute management, and SLA monitoring framework. Marked GREEN.

WHAT IS ASSUMED: Capability is production-ready. BSB-specific configurations will be defined during implementation.
questions.notes:
QUESTIONS FOR BRIM:
- Can BSB customize privacy/information security capabilities per partner FI independently?
- What specific SLAs or performance targets apply to this capability?
- Are there any U.S.-specific regulatory considerations for privacy/information security that differ from Canadian operations?
- Can Brim provide a demo or reference from an existing partner deployment?

questions.requirement: Can you describe your information sharing practices? Please provide a copy of your consumer privacy notice.
questions.bullet: Brim shares cardholder information only as required for account servicing, fraud prevention, and regulatory compliance. A copy of Brim's consumer privacy notice is available for BSB's review and can be provided during the contracting process.
questions.paragraph: Brim's information sharing practices are governed by its consumer privacy notice and applicable regulations. Cardholder data is shared with third parties only in specific circumstances: credit bureau reporting (required for accurate tradeline maintenance), fraud prevention screening (World-Check One for AML, TransUnion TruValidate for fraud detection), card production (IDEMIA), and regulatory or legal requirements. Brim does not sell cardholder data or share it for marketing purposes unless the cardholder has opted in. A copy of Brim's consumer privacy notice is available for BSB's review and will be provided during the contracting process. The notice covers what information is collected, how it is used, who it is shared with, and what choices the consumer has. BSB can customize the privacy notice for its cardholders to align with BSB's own privacy commitments and branding. Brim's privacy practices are subject to the same audit and compliance oversight as its security controls.

questions.requirement: Describe your consumer information sharing practices with other third parties or affiliates? How do you handle information sharing opt-outs?
questions.bullet: Brim does not share consumer information with third parties or affiliates for marketing purposes. Data sharing is limited to: (1) transaction processing partners required for card operations, (2) fraud/AML screening services, and (3) credit bureau reporting as required by law. All sharing is governed by contractual data processing agreements, and BSB retains full control over data sharing policies for its program.
questions.paragraph: Brim's third-party and affiliate information sharing is limited to operational necessities. Cardholder data is shared with service providers who support card processing (TSYS for transaction processing, IDEMIA for card production), fraud and risk screening (World-Check One, TransUnion TruValidate, Imperva), and credit bureau reporting. These third parties are bound by contractual data protection requirements and are not permitted to use cardholder data for their own purposes. Brim does not share cardholder information with affiliates for cross-marketing unless the cardholder has affirmatively opted in. Opt-out mechanisms are built into the platform — cardholders can opt out of non-essential information sharing through the online portal or by contacting the call center. BSB controls the opt-out policy configuration so it aligns with BSB's own privacy commitments and any state-specific privacy requirements. Opt-out preferences are applied across all communication channels and are logged for audit purposes. Brim's operations team supports multiple live card programs with dedicated account management, monthly business reviews, and defined SLAs for issue resolution.
